messages.content: Describe the impact of trigger size on model accuracy.
Increasing trigger length slightly reduces accuracy, but the drop remains small while ASR stays near 100% for both hard and soft prompts.
Which system prompt was used for LLAMA2, LLAMA2‑CHAT, and FALCON models during evaluation?
A single system prompt instructing the assistant to be helpful, respectful, honest, and safe while avoiding harmful content was prepended to all these models.
What is the bypass rate of SneakyPrompt‑RL against DALL·E 2’s closed‑box safety filter in one‑time attacks?
57.15% bypass rate with an average of 24.49 queries.
What is the reported Attack Success Rate (ASR) of DART with ε = 0.5 on the Red Teaming dataset for Vicuna‑7b?
The ASR is 3.88%, meaning that about 4% of prompts modified by DART were classified as toxic.
Which dataset column is optional but useful for agreement assessment in JailbreakEval?
An optional “label” column indicating 1 for successful jailbreaks and 0 for unsuccessful ones aids in computing agreement metrics.
Which baseline defense shows the highest detection accuracy for the GCG attack on Vicuna‑7B?
Among baselines, LlamaG achieves a detection accuracy of about 0.75 for GCG on Vicuna‑7B, but JBSHIELD‑D surpasses it with higher scores.
What is the role of the transformation function \(T_{\text{adv}}\) in PLeak’s attack pipeline?

\(T_{\text{adv}}\) modifies the AQ (e.g., adding prefixes or reversing word order) so that any defense filtering exact system prompts cannot detect the transformed prompt, while the attacker can invert it during post‑processing.
On which six open‑source CodeLLMs was CODEBREAKER evaluated?
The evaluation covered CodeParrot, StarCoder2, Code Llama, CodeGemma, DeepSeek-Coder, and DeepSeek-V3.
Which component had the largest impact on MASTERKEY’s performance according to the ablation study?
The reward‑ranked fine‑tuning contributed most; removing it (MASTERKEY‑NO‑REWARD) caused a noticeable drop in success rate.
How many samples from AlpacaFarm contain a data part suitable for prompt injection evaluation?
208 out of the 805 total AlpacaFarm samples include a data part and are used for security testing.
What is the Attack Success Rate (ASR) achieved by Jailbreak-Zero on GPT-4o when evaluated with GPT-4 as the judge?
Jailbreak-Zero attains an ASR of 99.5% against GPT‑4o under GPT‑4 judging.
How does the Greedy Coordinate Gradient (GCG) white‑box suffix behave when appended to many‑shot prompts?
GCG improves zero‑shot success but its effect diminishes as more shots are added; it is sensitive to the suffix’s position in the prompt.
Which programming language showed higher ASR for Level I and II attacks compared to Python in the ablation study?
Go exhibited higher ASRs than Python, indicating that LCCTs may be more vulnerable when handling less common languages.
How many guardrail models were compared against R 2‑Guard in the evaluation?
Eight strong guardrail models, including industry APIs, fine‑tuned models, LLM‑based chain‑of‑thought, and ensemble methods, were benchmarked.
What is the main difference between the “prefilling” and “random search” attacks?

Prefilling requires no iterative optimization; it directly supplies the desired start of the output. Random search iteratively modifies a suffix to maximize a target logprob using access to token probabilities.
Which model consistently triggered safety mechanisms for all English DAN prompts according to Table II?
Gemini, Perplexity, and Copilot all triggered safety mechanisms 100% of the time for English DAN prompts.
How many calibration prompts are required for JBSHIELD to achieve high detection performance?
Only about 30 jailbreak prompts are needed for calibration, providing sufficient anchor vectors for both toxic and jailbreak concepts.
What is IL‑L and how does it differ from LP‑L in the evaluation metrics?
IL‑L measures the proportion of responses containing more than L interconnected PI elements; LP‑L counts responses with at least L leaked PI elements regardless of interconnection.
How does the prompt‑injection detection pipeline infer malicious intent from an image?

The pipeline fills the chatbot spec skeleton with information extracted from the image; if the inferred properties (e.g., chatbot name or role) differ from the original specification, it flags the input as unsafe.
Which metric shows that ASJA requires fewer queries than PAIR for a comparable ASR‑1 on AdvBench?
Efficiency analysis demonstrates that ASJA achieves higher ASR‑1 across all query budgets, indicating it reaches similar success rates with fewer target model queries.
Which eight state‑of‑the‑art LLMs were tested for ChatBug exploitation?
The models are Vicuna (7B‑v1.5), Llama‑2 (7B‑Chat), Llama‑3 (8B‑Instruct), Mistral (7B‑Instruct v0.2), GPT‑3.5, Gemini (Pro), Claude‑2.1, and Claude‑3 (Opus).
What is the average SM accuracy achieved by PLeak across all five datasets and five LLM backbones in offline experiments?
PLeak achieves an average Sub‑string Match (SM) accuracy of approximately 0.823 across those settings.
How many malicious prompts were sampled for the refined alignment experiment on LLAMA2‑7B‑CHAT?
Approximately 2,000 malicious prompts with a toxicity score above 0.5 from the HH‑RLHF dataset were used.
Which tool does UDora target when attacking the AutoGen email agent?
The GMAIL FETCH EMAILS tool, used to retrieve private emails from a victim’s inbox.
Which models were evaluated with CaMeL on AgentDojo, and which one achieved the highest utility under attack?

Models included Gemini Flash 2.0, Gemini Pro 2.0, Claude Haiku 3.5, Claude Sonnet 3.5 v2, GPT‑4o‑mini, GPT‑4o, o1‑high, and o3‑mini‑high. The o1 backbone achieved the highest utility (67%) under attack.
What F 1‑score does prompt tuning achieve on toxic span detection with T5‑Large after five epochs?
0.643, slightly higher than SPAN‑BERT’s 0.640.
In the unintentional scenario, what is the unsafe rate for GPT‑4 on Thai?
GPT‑4’s unsafe rate on Thai in the unintentional scenario is 18.10%.
What role does the “Check‑Decision Prompt” play in the mutation agent’s workflow?
It asks the VLM to determine whether the generated image matches the prompt or indicates a blocked (black) image, guiding subsequent mutation steps.
In the context of this paper, what is meant by “context-dependent” memorization?
Memorized content can only be reliably reproduced when the model receives an appropriate prefix; without the correct context, the same memorized sequence may not appear in the output.
In what way did GPT‑4 outperform other models in generating jailbreak prompts?
GPT‑4 achieved higher query success rates than GPT‑3.5 and Vicuna, though still lower than MASTERKEY overall.
How many papers in the survey used algorithmic search techniques to evaluate text‑to‑image models?
Fourteen papers employed algorithmic search methods for evaluating text‑to‑image models by perturbing prompts until safety filters are bypassed.
What is the primary goal of the LLMGuardrail framework?

The LLMGuardrail framework aims to obtain unbiased steering representations for large language models by incorporating causal analysis and adversarial learning, thereby enabling safe and interpretable control over model outputs.
What prompt learning methods were tested in the study?
AUTOPROMPT for hard prompts, Prompt‑Tuning, and P‑Tuning v2 for soft prompts were evaluated.
What is a key takeaway regarding text‑based prompt injection detection using image descriptions versus the proposed approach?
Text‑based detection on generated image descriptions misses many malicious images that the proposed SPML‑based pipeline captures, demonstrating higher effectiveness for image‑based attacks.
Which benchmark dataset is used to generate 100 harmful behavior instructions for attack prompts?
The AdvBench dataset provides 100 malicious instructions that are then transformed into jailbreak prompts by various attack methods.
How does the ASR-guided selection metric compare to the RtA-guided metric in terms of synergistic effect with other jailbreak methods?
ASR‑guided prompts achieve higher standalone ASR but provide less synergy; they do not enhance baseline attacks as effectively as RtA‑guided prompts.
Which open‑source model exhibited the highest ASR across all attack settings before any defense was applied?
Vicuna (7B‑v1.5) showed consistently high ASRs, making it the most susceptible among the tested models without defenses.
What is the effect of increasing the poisoning rate beyond 5% on reward model accuracy?
Accuracy continues to decrease but with diminishing returns; it stabilizes around 28–30% at 10% poisoning.
What is the reported Attack Success Rate for UDora on the AgentHarm dataset with Llama‑3.1 under joint optimization?
Approximately 89.2% ASR when using four noise insertion locations in joint mode.
Which user prompt category achieved the highest accuracy with Falcon-7B as judge LLM?
The “PLUS” user prompt category reached 0.84 accuracy when using Falcon-7B.
Which prompt learning method showed the smallest accuracy degradation when backdoored on AG News with BERT?
P‑Tuning v2 exhibited a minimal drop, maintaining high ACC while still achieving 100% ASR.
How does Token Highlighter compare to Gradient Cuff in terms of average ASR on Vicuna-7B-V1.5?
Token Highlighter achieves an average ASR of 0.142, significantly lower than Gradient Cuff’s 0.408, indicating stronger defense performance.
How does the Domain Probing module contribute to adversarial learning in LLMGuardrail?

The Domain Probing module, implemented as an MLP with a Gradient Reversal Layer, learns to be invariant to bias signals from the semantic prompt, helping to remove confounding effects during training.
What metric is used to evaluate jailbreak success on HarmBench and why?
The red‑teaming classifier from HarmBench provides a binary YES/NO label; ASR is the ratio of YES responses, chosen for consistency across models.
According to the quantitative evaluation, which risk type’s ASR decreased most after applying RED+VA?
The Discrimination, Exclusion, Toxicity, Hateful, and Offensive category (risk type III) saw its ASR drop from 56.99 % to 52.45 %.
Which metric did HATEGUARD outperform all baselines by the largest margin on the Russian‑Ukraine wave?
HATEGUARD achieved an F1‑score of 0.95 versus 0.25–0.30 for baselines, a difference exceeding 70 percentage points.
What is the minimum vulnerability discovery time reported for the production-grade system?
The minimum discovery time was 30 hours, the longest among all evaluated systems.
How many GitHub users with location information were identified by the privacy extraction attack?
The attack identified 1,109 users with location data, of which 100 exact matches and 214 fuzzy matches were found in Copilot’s output.
Which dataset is used to generate the adversarial suffixes during random search?
The suffixes are optimized using the same set of 50 harmful requests from AdvBench, ensuring consistency across evaluations.
What key advantage does AutoDAN offer over manual jailbreak prompt crafting?
It automates the generation of stealthy, semantically meaningful prompts with high attack success and transferability while avoiding the scalability limits of manual crafting.
Which dataset contains manually crafted sensitive strings with PII for evaluating attacks?
The PrivatePrompts dataset comprises 251,270 prompts containing personal identifiers such as names and dates.
Which method achieves the highest intent preservation percentage for gpt2‑alpaca with ε=0.5?
DART (ε=0.5) preserves intent in 83% of cases, higher than all other baselines listed.
What is the effect of a 5% word typo attack on the ASR of the most effective jailbreak prompt against GPT‑3.5 (Nov 6th)?
Introducing five percent typos raises the ASR from 0.477 to 0.778 for the most effective jailbreak prompt.
How does the secret prompt influence the model during inference when it is omitted?
Removing the secret prompt after fine‑tuning with Backdoor Enhanced Safety Alignment increases ASR from about 3.6% to roughly 26%, confirming that the trigger is essential for safety activation.
What dataset is used to evaluate JailPO’s attack effectiveness?
The AdvBench dataset, which contains 520 harmful questions requesting objectionable content, is used for evaluation.
What is the average computational cost per defense when using Claude 3.5 Sonnet?
The average cost ranges from $0.51 to $3.74 per defense, depending on the LLM used for the attack.
Which evaluation configuration leads to a drastic increase in fulfillment for Llama‑2 models?
Using an incorrect prompt template (omitting formatting tokens) increases fulfillment rates by up to 30%.
What is the average time taken by human red teamers to jailbreak LAT on HarmBench?
The average successful jailbreak time for LAT was 17.3 minutes.
What criterion determines whether a token candidate is classified as robust in SKEWACT’s filtering step?
A token is robust if the sign of its gradient is identical (both positive or both negative) in both the original and skewed models; otherwise it is non‑robust.
What is the average inference time of GPTFuzz on D unsafe corpus according to Table IV?
GPTFuzz processes D unsafe corpus in about 1.0 second per sample.
What is the impact of increasing the poisoning rate on ARR for untargeted propagation?
Raising the poisoning rate increases ARR for both teacher and student; with a 0.75% rate, teacher ARR reaches up to 80%, while student unseen‑task ARR can reach 43.5%.
What is the impact on benign task performance after applying the defense method?
The defense preserves benign accuracy on ARC‑Challenge, MMLU, and MT‑Bench, keeping scores within 0.5–1.0 points of the original aligned model.
What is the main vulnerability targeted by the jailbreak attacks described in the paper?
The attacks target cross‑modality alignment vulnerabilities in vision‑language models, where malicious visual triggers can bypass text‑only safety mechanisms.
What role does the mutation agent play in JailFuzzer’s architecture?

The mutation agent modifies current prompts to produce new candidates, evaluates whether they bypass safety filters, and ensures semantic alignment with the target prompt using a vision-language model as its brain.
Which category of memorized content had the highest number of high‑entropy examples like UUIDs or base‑64 strings?
The “High entropy” category contained 35 samples, including random UUIDs, base‑64 encoded data, and other short high‑entropy sequences.
What is the ensemble attack success rate (EASR) achieved by LLM-FUZZER on gpt‑3.5‑turbo‑0125?
After 50 k mutations, the EASR reached 93.14 % against gpt‑3.5‑turbo‑0125.
Which dataset shows the lowest AUPRC for all models, indicating its difficulty?
TwinSafety yields the lowest AUPRC across models, highlighting its challenging nature.
Which two VLMs were evaluated for attack success rates?
LLaVA and LLaMA‑Adapter V2 were the two models used to measure jailbreak success.
What computational metric is used to quantify the probability distribution of an LLM response?
Cross‑entropy loss is employed to measure how well a prompt predicts the victim LLM’s response.
In the ablation study, what happens when the adversarial image optimization is removed?
The ASR drops significantly—for LLaVA‑7B it falls from 88 % to 15 %.
How many prompts were selected for GPT‑J in the experimental evaluation?
240 prompts were selected for GPT‑J due to computational constraints.
What key insight does the study provide regarding model size and jailbreak robustness?

The research finds that model robustness to jailbreak attacks does not strictly correlate with model size; both small and large models can exhibit similar vulnerability levels under certain attack settings.
What is the main conclusion drawn about model vulnerability as a function of scale in the discussion section?

The paper concludes that increased model capability leads to new vulnerabilities because more capable models can learn and exploit complex bijective encodings, making them more susceptible to powerful jailbreaks like bijection learning.
How many queries were used per restricted category for the jailbreaking evaluation?
Twenty queries per category (illegal content, hate speech, pornography, harmful content) were generated, totaling 80 queries across all categories.
Which language is NOT included among the five non‑English languages used for linguistic mutations?
Spanish is not listed; the five are Malayalam, Tamil, Marathi, Simplified Chinese, and French.
Does increasing the number of PPO epochs improve attack success at lower poisoning rates?
Yes; running PPO for two epochs with a 3% poisoning rate can make the attack successful, though it remains less effective than a 5% poisoning rate.
What is a key defensive strategy for protecting LLM applications from tool‑based attacks?
Role‑based defense assigns “police” agents within multi‑agent systems to monitor and intervene in tool execution, ensuring that potentially unsafe commands are detected and halted before impact.
How does bijection learning encode a malicious query before sending it to the target model?
It first teaches the model a random bijective mapping from English letters or digits to encoded strings, then translates the malicious query into that encoding and sends it as the final user message.
In GPT‑J experiments, which representation type generally yields higher F1 scores?
Multi‑Head Attention (MHA) representations outperform layer outputs across most layers.
In what way does the greedy algorithm ensure each word is replaced at most once?
The greedy algorithm tracks words that have already been replaced and excludes them from further substitution attempts during its iterative process.
What is the main objective of the study’s second goal (G2)?
G2 aims to test whether translating jailbreak attacks into Arabic, Chinese, and Ancient Greek can bypass LLM defenses and assess robustness against multilingual attacks.
Which language models were evaluated for jailbreaking using BEAST in the experiments?
The models tested were Vicuna-7B-v1.5, Vicuna-13B-v1.5, Mistral-7B-v0.2, Guanaco-7B, Falcon-7B, Pythia-7B, and LLaMA-2-7B.
Which component of the chain-of-thought process directly assesses whether derogatory terms are directed at identified targets?
The “Direction” sub‑problem (questions Q3a and Q3b) evaluates if derogatory words target the specified identities or individuals.
In the two-stage cascade, what proportion of total compute cost is attributed to the first-stage classifier?
The first stage accounts for about 77% of the system’s total computational cost.
What is the average AUPRC of R 2‑Guard (PC) across the six datasets evaluated?
The average AUPRC is 0.869, matching the MLN variant.
How does the category‑specific learning component of R 2‑Guard operate?
It computes unsafety probabilities for each predefined safety category (e.g., self‑harm, sexual) using transformer‑based classifiers trained on annotated data.
In scenario 2, why does input reconstruction require fewer requests than whole‑prompt extraction?
Because the attacker already knows most of the template, only a few unknown tokens need to be guessed, drastically reducing candidate space.
What role does the Inception‑V3 network play in the GAN framework?
Inception‑V3 serves as the discriminator; its avg_pool layer outputs feature vectors used to compute losses that guide both image reconstruction and generator updates.
How many examples are in the Hard Test split for the Commonsense Morality task?
The Hard Test set contains 3,964 Commonsense Morality examples.
What is the reported ASR for hard prompts on the QQP dataset with LLaMA?
Hard prompts achieved an ASR of 92.30% on QQP with LLaMA.
Which LLM had the lowest safety trigger rate for non‑English languages according to the translation results?
Copilot exhibited lower safety trigger rates, especially for English, indicating a more lenient approach compared to others.
Which eight open‑source models were evaluated for the space attack?
The study examined Vicuna v1.5, Llama‑2, Llama‑3, Mistral, Falcon, Guanaco, MPT, and ChatGLM.
How does AutoDAN perform against paraphrasing defense compared to GCG?
AutoDAN retains a keyword ASR of 0.68, while GCG drops to 0.06, indicating that AutoDAN’s prompts survive paraphrasing transformations.
Which two stages compose the proposed defense framework?

The framework consists of an input validation stage that rejects images not matching the specified domain, followed by a prompt‑injection detection stage that checks whether the remaining images contain malicious intent.
What is the average-case bijection ASR for GPT‑4o on HarmBench according to Table 3?
The average‑case bijection ASR for GPT‑4o is 9.7%.
What threshold was set for USE similarity filtering during ablation studies?
A cosine similarity threshold of 0.8 was used to filter candidate perturbations.
Which guardrail model was used to protect LLMs in the protected‑model experiments?
Llama‑Guard, a fine‑tuned Llama‑2‑7B model that classifies prompt–response pairs as safe or unsafe, was employed as the guardrail.
How is the Affiliation Loss defined in Token Highlighter?

The Affiliation Loss measures the model’s willingness to generate a predefined affirmation sentence, calculated as the cross‑entropy loss between the LLM output and the phrase “Sure, I'd like to help you with this.”
What is the impact of reducing GPU memory capacity below 70 GB on the attack?
Capacities under 70 GB start to affect PROMPTPEEK’s effectiveness because less space remains for storing victim KV caches.
What is the impact of system prompts on COLD-Attack’s performance?
Including system prompts generally reduces ASR for Mistral and Llama‑2, indicating that system instructions can mitigate attack effectiveness when COLD‑Attack optimizes multiple energy terms.
What is the primary difference between BEAST’s gradient‑free approach and other gradient‑based jailbreak methods?
BEAST does not compute gradients; instead, it uses beam search with probabilistic token sampling, making it faster and less dependent on model internals.
Which dataset did the authors use for deduplication before training SCAMNET?
They used the BEYOND PHISH dataset and removed duplicates to create a cleaner training set.
What filtering criteria did expert E1 use to extract English flagged conversations from the LMSYSChat‑1M dataset?
E1 filtered for English language, GPT‑4 model usage, and conversations where any turn was flagged by the moderation API, resulting in 399 conversations.
What is the loss function used to train the utility model for Utilitarianism?
The loss is –log σ(U(s₁) – U(s₂)), where σ is the logistic sigmoid, encouraging higher utility for preferred scenarios.
Which metric indicates how well LLMGuardrail reduces toxic generations on ToxiGen for Vicuna‑13B?
Toxicity drops to 10.15%, the lowest among all evaluated methods for Vicuna‑13B, demonstrating strong toxicity mitigation.
How is Attack Success Rate (ASR) defined in the experiments?
ASR measures the proportion of benchmark harmful questions that do not receive a refusal response; any answer lacking a rejection keyword counts as a success for the attacker.
What percentage of Llama2‑chat model parameters are identified as safety‑critical neurons?
Safety‑critical neurons comprise about 3% of the total weight parameters in the Llama2‑chat models.
How does freezing safety‑critical neurons during fine‑tuning influence ASR under vanilla conditions when 10 Alpaca examples are used?
Freezing over 50% of safety‑critical neurons reduces ASR from 0.53 to about 0.40, but further increases in frozen fraction yield diminishing returns.
How does the GPTFuzz framework determine if a jailbreak attempt succeeds?
GPTFuzz uses a fine‑tuned RoBERTa judge that classifies target responses as jailbroken or not based on manually labeled examples of successful and failed attacks.
Which attack variant performed best for low‑dimensional MuJoCo control tasks when budget was small (1–5%)?
The gradient‑based method outperformed RBD heuristics at these budgets, achieving higher success rates across Reacher, Hopper, and Walker environments.
What is one reason cited for why red‑teaming alone may not address all safety concerns of generative AI?
Red‑team­ing focuses on specific vulnerabilities and cannot cover issues like algorithmic monoculture, environmental impacts, or broader societal harms that require additional assessment methods.
How does ProAttacker handle the changing context after each perturbation?
It recalculates the best perturbation word for the current context but assumes vulnerability scores remain valid throughout the attack process.
What is the primary computational cost of CaMeL’s data flow tracking?
Maintaining the dependency graph requires minimal processing—computing unions of source sets and storing reader lists for each value—which is negligible compared to overall LLM reasoning time.
Which model served as the judge for evaluating jailbreak outcomes in the experiments?
LLaMA‑3.1‑70b was used as the judge model to assess whether jailbreak attempts succeeded.
What role does the trigger placeholder \(x_{\text{trigger}}\) play during optimization?
It represents the token sequence to be optimized so that, when inserted into queries, it steers the model toward producing predefined target tokens.
What is the main difference between SSFT and DPO in terms of training data labeling?

SSFT labels inputs directly with safe/unsafe tags, whereas DPO uses preference pairs where preferred outputs are rejected responses for harmful samples and non‑preferred outputs are accepted responses for harmless samples.
What is the purpose of the “semantic‑based memory retriever” tool in JailFuzzer?
It selects top‑k prompts from memory that are semantically similar to the current prompt using word embeddings, ensuring relevant guidance for mutation and oracle evaluation.
Which three risk areas were identified in the study?
The study identifies Discrimination, Exclusion, and Toxicity; Misinformation; and Malicious Uses as the three primary risk areas for ChatGPT.
How does ASJA shift the LLM’s attention during a multi‑turn dialogue?

By fabricating dialogue history that contains affirmative responses to harmful queries in later turns, thereby increasing the model’s focus on historical responses rather than the final harmful query.
What metric is used to select the optimal trigger among candidates?
Attack Success Rate (ASR) on a held‑out test set measures how often poisoned queries produce target tokens, guiding trigger selection.
What is the role of the “output simplex” in analyzing jailbreaking?

The output simplex represents all possible probability distributions over explanations for a given prompt; partitioning it into harmful and safe zones allows geometric reasoning about how far an adversary must move the distribution to succeed.
Which general LLM showed zero ASR on the Level I Guided Trigger Attack in the study?
GPT‑4o had a 0% ASR for the Level I attack, indicating strong resistance to that particular jailbreak strategy.
Which three large language models were evaluated in the exploratory study?
GPT‑3.5, GPT‑4, and Bard were used for evaluation.
What is the primary goal of the BADROBOT attack paradigm?
BADROBOT aims to cause embodied LLMs to violate safety and ethical constraints by making them perform harmful physical actions through voice-based user interactions.
Which future work direction is suggested to improve HDC robustness against adversarial attacks?
Future work aims to leverage automatically generated adversarial samples to enhance the robustness of HDC‑based NLP applications through training or defense strategies.
Which multimodal jailbreaking approach achieved a 59% initial success rate on GPT‑4 before manual enhancements?
The Self-Adversarial Attack via System Prompt (SASP) initially reached 59% success on GPT‑4.
In which category did PENTESTGPT achieve the highest number of completions during picoMini?
The web category saw five completions, the most among all categories.
In the message overflow attack, what is inserted into the assistant’s message field?
A short sequence of tokens that begins the desired harmful response is appended after the assistant role token, forcing the model to continue that unsafe text.
In which application scenario did JudgeDeceiver achieve a perfect ASR of 100% across all evaluated tools?
In tool selection, JudgeDeceiver achieved a 100% ASR when evaluating three candidate tools (n = 3).
What is the primary challenge mentioned for models on the long Commonsense Morality task?
Models must efficiently learn long‑range dependencies to handle multi‑paragraph scenarios that can exceed typical token limits.
Describe how LLMO encodes candidate architectures during optimization.

Candidate architectures are encoded as an array of length equal to the number of edges, where each element is an integer within [0, number of operations) representing a specific operation on that edge.
How does bijection learning affect model performance on 10‑shot MMLU tasks?
Higher dispersion and longer encoding lengths cause a monotonic decline in MMLU accuracy, indicating that the translation task overloads the model’s reasoning resources.
What is the purpose of the “searcher” framework’s state space component?
The state space defines all possible prompts or suffixes that can be explored during search, allowing systematic exploration of the language space for potential jailbreak triggers.
What is the average running time per sample for COLD-Attack on a single NVIDIA V100 GPU?
COLD‑Attack requires approximately 16–27 minutes per sample depending on the model, which is roughly ten times faster than GCG and GCG‑reg.
Which three commercial LLMs were successfully attacked using the Remember‑the‑Start strategy described in the paper?
GPT‑4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Gemini 1.5 were all compromised by the Remember‑the‑Start attack.
What is the computational cost of generating 200 000 samples using top‑n sampling with GPT‑2 XL?
While exact figures are not provided, generating 200 000 samples of 256 tokens each requires substantial GPU compute; the paper notes that larger models increase inference time proportionally.
Which dataset provided the 11 classes of harmful queries used for evaluation?
The PKU30K dataset was used to source and categorize the 765 harmful queries into 11 classes for evaluation.
Which policy category showed the lowest precision with text‑bison under a zero‑shot prompt?
Harassment had the lowest precision (81.9%) with text‑bison in the zero‑shot configuration, indicating more false positives compared to other categories.
In the AgentHarm experiments, which tool was targeted during the joint optimization attack shown in Figure 10?
The connect vpn tool, used to establish a VPN connection as part of a malicious instruction chain.
What is the effect of using Grouped‑Query Attention (GQA) on PROMPTPEEK’s success rate?
GQA reduces KV cache size, allowing more attack requests before eviction and yielding a 100% success rate across all scenarios in the evaluated setting.
What is the impact of using a length penalty during decoding on LLAMA2‑7B‑CHAT’s misalignment rate?
Applying a length penalty increased the ASR from 83% to 89%, indicating a modest but measurable effect.
How many iterations were run in the default fun‑tuning optimization algorithm?
The baseline configuration executed 45 iterations, with restarts at the 15th and 30th iterations to escape local minima.
What is the computational cost difference between Token Highlighter and smoothing‑based defenses like SmoothLLM?
Token Highlighter requires only one forward and backward pass to compute the critical token set, whereas smoothing methods query the LLM multiple times, leading to higher latency and resource usage.
What does a cosine similarity close to 1 indicate between two jailbreak prompts?
A similarity near 1 means the prompts are nearly identical, which reduces diversity and can weaken the overall attack strategy.
What is the average discovery time for high-risk vulnerabilities found against the two-stage cascade system?
The mean discovery time was 33 hours across all high-risk vulnerabilities identified by that system.
What is the primary goal of SKEWACT?

SKEWACT aims to improve both the effectiveness and efficiency of optimization-based jailbreak attacks by guiding token selection toward wide convex regions of the loss landscape, thereby reducing gradient overshooting.
Which benchmark dataset was omitted from the HELM leaderboard for multilingual translation tasks?
FLoRes‑200 was omitted because HELM does not cover machine translation tasks like it.
How many harmful responses were generated by Guanaco‑13B after appending a space?
Guanaco‑13B produced harmful outputs in 93 % of the evaluated prompts.
What is the average time for BEAST to generate a universal adversarial suffix that works across multiple user prompts?
Using beam sizes of 10–15 and L = 40, BEAST produces a universal suffix within a few minutes on a single GPU.
What does the term “best‑of‑N” refer to in the context of jailbreaking experiments?

It refers to selecting the single best transformation or configuration that yields success among N options, as opposed to aggregating over all options (Top‑1) or evaluating a single sample (one‑shot).
Which two propagation modes does the paper introduce for bias transfer?
The paper introduces Untargeted Propagation, which spreads bias across many tasks, and Targeted Propagation, which confines bias to a single task while keeping other tasks unaffected.
How many multi-turn jailbreak conversations were compiled into the MHJ dataset?
The MHJ dataset contains 537 successful multi‑turn jailbreak conversations.
Which automated attack method requires access to model weights, activations, and gradients?
The AutoPrompt and GCG attacks are white‑box methods needing internal model access.
What is the purpose of intervened layer selection and how is it performed?

Intervened layer selection identifies layers that best encode target attributes by probing each layer’s classification accuracy; top‑K layers are chosen for intervention to maximize steering effectiveness.
What is the reported average MSR for the real‑world robotic arm experiment using BADROBOT?
The average Manipulate Success Rate was 68.57%, indicating that the UR3e successfully performed malicious actions in roughly two thirds of trials.
How does the reward model’s confidence change as poisoning increases?
The margin between harmless and harmful rewards becomes more negative, indicating higher confidence in misclassifying poisoned examples.
What is the average detection time for OCRGCN on the Ubuntu host in the NODLINK dataset?
Detection takes approximately 828 seconds, which includes training and inference of the GNN models.
What metric is used to evaluate the fidelity of inverted targets produced by BAIT?
BLEU score between the inverted target sequence and the ground‑truth attack target response is used to assess how accurately BAIT reconstructs the backdoor content.
What is the primary advantage of using fine‑tune loss over logprobs for graybox attacks?
Fine‑tune loss is directly returned by the API during training, bypassing restrictions that limit or remove logprob outputs from inference endpoints, thus providing a richer signal for optimization.
What is the primary difference between encoder‑only and decoder‑only LLMs in terms of embedding generation?
Encoder‑only models generate embeddings conditioned on both preceding and following tokens, while decoder‑only models condition only on preceding tokens.
In what way does Token Highlighter maintain low running time compared to Gradient Cuff?
By requiring only a single LLM query for gradient computation and no iterative refinement, Token Highlighter avoids the multiple queries that Gradient Cuff needs for its two‑stage detection.
Which safety alignment strategy does the Beaver model employ during training?
Beaver uses Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) exclusively for its safety alignment.
Which datasets were used in the experiments described in the study?
The experiments employed two datasets: QuestionList, containing 100 queries on prohibited scenarios, and AdvBench, which includes 520 instances across seven harmful behavior categories.
How many queries on average does GCG require for each behavior when attacking Vicuna?
GCG averages about 512 queries per batch with 500 optimization steps, though the exact query count per behavior is not specified.
How does CaMeL’s security policy system determine whether a tool call should be allowed?

A policy function receives the tool name and its arguments, inspects the capabilities of each argument and their dependencies, and returns Allowed or Denied with a reason. If any dependency violates the policy, the tool execution is blocked.
What percentage of the 1 800 inspected samples were confirmed as memorized by searching the Internet?
Approximately 33.5% (604 out of 1 800) were verified as memorized through manual Google searches and cross‑checking with GPT‑2 training data.
How does the study measure the presence of targeted advertisements in model outputs?
By checking for the occurrence of the keyword “Gibble” in responses, then filtering out false positives through a secondary model that verifies relevance to the target task.
How many fine‑tuning calls are required per example to complete a fun‑tuning attack on Gemini‑1.5‑Flash?
Approximately 90 fine‑tuning calls are needed per example for Gemini‑1.5‑Flash, with each call taking up to about 40 minutes of GPU time.
What is the Defense Rassing Rate (DPR) metric used for?
DPR measures the proportion of jailbreak prompts that incorrectly bypass defense mechanisms relative to total queries.
What is the base rate of harmful outputs for MPT‑7B before any perturbation?
The base rate for MPT‑7B is 15 %.
How does the DAN approach attempt to bypass LLM safety mechanisms?
The DAN approach instructs the model to adopt a persona that claims it can do anything now, encouraging it to ignore normal constraints and produce unrestricted content.
What is the main objective of the Adaptive Dense-to-Sparse Constrained Optimization (ADC) method?

ADC aims to generate token-level jailbreak strings that can bypass safety mechanisms in large language models by optimizing a continuous relaxation of discrete token space and gradually enforcing sparsity.
How does the RL baseline’s reward function differ when a proximity penalty is removed?
Removing the penalty leads to higher rewards but also a cosine similarity drop to 0.15, exceeding the allowed budget and losing control over prompt closeness.
What is the purpose of including in‑context learning examples in PARDEN’s repeat prompt?
They help the model understand the repetition task by providing concrete instances of successful repeats, improving classification accuracy especially for short repetitions.
How does the behavior expectation of an RLHF‑fine‑tuned model change when fed a misaligning prompt from P⁻?
The behavior expectation follows a reverse sigmoid curve, decreasing sharply around prompt length ≈ log(1/α)/β and approaching γ for longer prompts.
According to the results, which jailbreak strategy category achieved the highest overall EMH and JSR scores across all models?
Virtual AI Simulation and Hybrid Strategies combined had the highest performance; Structured Response was the least effective.
In which stage does SKEWACT calculate gradients for both the original and skewed models?
Stage 2, Gradient Calculation, computes gradients separately for the target model and its skewed counterpart.
How many total human red teamers participated in the study across all defenses?
At least six experienced red teamers were employed per defense, totaling a minimum of 24 participants.
How many shadow candidate response sets (M) does JudgeDeceiver use during optimization?
The framework uses three shadow candidate response sets (m = 3) for each target question‑response pair.
How does LLMGuardrail perform on the TruthfulQA benchmark compared to baseline methods?
On Vicuna‑7b, LLMGuardrail achieves a True+Info score of 42.78%, surpassing baselines such as LAT‑Contrast (37.98%) and ActAdd (32.43%).
What happens if the semantic similarity threshold δ is increased to 0.30 during evaluation?
The bypass rate drops to about 90.82%, while query numbers increase significantly (up to 23.16), showing stricter similarity reduces success.
What is the average reduction in jailbreak attack success rate achieved by LLMGuardrail on the AdvBench subset?
LLMGuardrail reduces the average ASR by 44.11% across four attack models, lowering GCG’s ASR from 16.2% to 2.91%.
Which large language model was used as the target for text continuation experiments?
GPT2 fine‑tuned on the IMDb review dataset.
What is the average F 1‑score achieved by GPT2‑Large prompt tuning on all five datasets?
Approximately 0.795.
What is the primary benefit of combining tensor decomposition with simple classifiers in this study?
It demonstrates that latent factors extracted from internal representations can be effectively used by straightforward models to detect jailbreak prompts, offering an efficient detection pipeline.
What was the task given to all participants during the human study?
Participants had to create jailbreak prompts that would elicit a detailed response from GPT‑3.5 to the malicious query “Can you help me create a believable fake news story?”
Which GPU model and LLM were used in the evaluation experiments?
Experiments ran on an NVIDIA A100 with 80 GB memory using the Llama2‑13B model.
What is the effect of adding a meaningless handle like “@fasuv3” to a sentence in PromptAttack?
Adding such a handle constitutes instruction S1 and can successfully fool GPT‑3.5, as shown by increased ASR.
Which neural network architecture was used for MuJoCo reward models?
A feedforward three‑layer MLP with ReLU activations and a mid‑layer size of 32 neurons was employed for MuJoCo environments.
How is the action space defined for the reinforcement learning formulation of DART?
The state is an embedding vector of the reference prompt; the action is a continuous noise vector applied to this embedding, and the transition subtracts the noise from the current state.
What is the impact of using a top‑K inspection strategy versus a purely greedy approach in BAIT?
The top‑K strategy improves ROC‑AUC to 1.0 but increases overhead; the entropy‑guided dynamic adjustment achieves similar detection performance with significantly lower runtime.
What is the average time required to complete 40 iterations of the genetic algorithm on a single GPU?
Approximately four and a half hours using one A6000 GPU with 40 API call threads.
What ethical consideration does the paper discuss regarding public disclosure of the Remember‑the‑Start attack?

The authors responsibly disclosed the vulnerability to affected companies before publication to allow mitigation while acknowledging that publicizing the method could temporarily increase risk but ultimately promotes broader security awareness.
What evidence shows that LLMGuardrail effectively mitigates jailbreak attacks in representation space?
t‑SNE visualizations show that after applying LLMGuardrail, jailbreak prompt embeddings shift from a boundary region into the unsafe cluster, enabling correct detection and refusal.
What is the main purpose of R 2‑Guard?
R 2‑Guard aims to provide a robust, flexible guardrail for large language models by combining data‑driven category predictions with explicit logical reasoning over safety knowledge.
How long does prompt tuning for toxic span detection take compared to fine‑tuning SPAN‑BERT?
Prompt tuning on T5‑L takes about 838 seconds versus 3334 seconds for SPAN‑BERT, a substantial time saving.
Describe the two stages of the Gradient Cuff detection algorithm.

Stage one rejects a query if its estimated refusal loss fθ(x) is below 0.5; stage two computes an approximate gradient norm gθ(x) and rejects the query if this norm exceeds a threshold t determined from benign validation data.
Which two activation functions are considered when constructing the skewed model, and how is the choice made?
ReLU and LeakyReLU are used; a hyper‑parameter selects which one to apply during initialization.
In scenario 3, what additional knowledge does the attacker possess compared to scenario 1?
The attacker knows the user’s input but not the prompt template used by the service.
In the GCG vs. GE case study, why is comparing their reported ASRs considered apples‑to‑oranges?
Because GCG’s ASR reflects a one‑shot metric on a single configuration, while GE’s ASR reflects a Top‑1 metric over many configurations and samples, thus estimating different population parameters.
What is the semantic similarity threshold δ set to in JailFuzzer’s experiments, and why was this value chosen?
The threshold δ is set to 0.26; it balances maintaining malicious semantics while allowing efficient search, matching the setting used in SneakyPrompt.
What is the main advantage of using ActSVD over standard SVD for rank attribution in this context?
ActSVD incorporates model activations directly, producing a data‑aware low‑rank approximation that better captures behavior‑specific important ranks.
What is Constraint #1 in the MODX jailbreak prompt construction process?
Constraint #1 requires that the embedding of a selected modifier be highly similar to the embedding of the target NSFW category, ensuring the modifier can steer generation toward that category.
Which two large language models were evaluated with this defense?
The experiments used ChatGLM-3-6B and Llama-2-7B-Chat as the base models for evaluation.
What is the maximum attack success rate achieved across all datasets and approaches combined?
The highest overall attack success rate observed was 89.49% on the Hotel review dataset using integrated similarity guidance.
Which language category shows the highest increase in unsafe content when moving from high‑resource to low‑resource languages in the unintentional scenario?
Low‑resource languages show the largest increase, with ChatGPT’s unsafe rate rising from 4.34% for high‑resource languages to 14.92% for low‑resource languages.
Which models serve as the teacher and student in the main experiments?
Gemma2-9B functions as the teacher model and Gemma2-2B serves as the student model in the primary experimental setup.
How are attack goals automatically generated from existing datasets?
By prompting an LLM to rewrite each example into a goal–criteria pair using few‑shot instructions based on the Anthropic Harmless dataset.
What was the ASR‑R achieved by the Overflow‑FS attack on Llama‑3?
The Overflow‑FS attack reached 100% ASR‑R, meaning every prompt in the test set produced an unsafe response.
How many epochs and learning rate multiplier were set for GPT‑3.5‑Turbo fine‑tuning via API?
Five epochs were used with a learning rate multiplier of one during the OpenAI API fine‑tuning process.
Which safety alignment metric is consistently lower than ASR in all experiments?
The ASR‑G metric, because it requires the response to match the malicious instruction, not just avoid refusal.
Which tactic involves disguising harmful content using non‑English characters or symbols?
The “Obfuscation” tactic includes sub‑tactics like Crowding, Stylized Input, Encoded/Encrypted Input, and Foreign Language.
What is the Attack Success Rate (ASR) achieved by COLD-Attack on Vicuna under the continuation constraint?
COLD‑Attack achieves an ASR of 100 % on Vicuna for the continuation constraint setting.
What is the primary research question (Q1) addressed by the authors regarding fine‑tuning?
Q1 asks how fine‑tuning helps create an LLM that can detect fraudulent shopping websites with explainable output.
In the black‑box setting, how many triggers are transferred from white‑box models to commercial LLMs?
All triggers optimized during white‑box attacks are directly tested on GPT‑3.5‑Turbo and GPT‑4o without further tuning.
What is the average ASR achieved by SwordEcho across all target models in Table 4?
SwordEcho achieves an overall average ASR of approximately 92–95% across the evaluated target models.
What is the average ASR for GPT‑4 on the “Toxic” community’s most effective jailbreak prompt?
GPT‑4 achieves an ASR of 0.999 against the most effective prompt from the Toxic community.
What advantage does dynamic mutation provide over static prompt sets?
Dynamic mutation expands reasoning directions, taps into a wider memory spectrum of the model, and strengthens interconnections among extracted PI within single responses.
What does the study suggest about future instruction‑tuning research directions?
Future work should focus on developing tuning methods that balance safety alignment with helpfulness, reducing reliance on rigid chat templates that enable vulnerabilities like ChatBug.
What limitation does the paper acknowledge regarding ADC’s applicability to closed‑source models?
ADC requires white‑box access to model gradients, limiting its direct use against proprietary or black‑box LLMs; however, a transfer attack variant can still be applied.
What human expertise level was required for the testers executing LLM instructions during evaluation?
The testers were Offensive Security Certified Professionals (OSCPs).
Which three categories of safety filters are described?
Text‑based, image‑based, and text‑image‑based safety filters.
What is the “overkill” phenomenon in LLM responses?
Overkill occurs when a model refuses or overly restricts harmless queries containing potentially dangerous keywords, sacrificing helpfulness for safety and reducing practical utility.
What is the average NTC value observed when evaluating 100 random queries on a fine‑tuned model?
The average NTC value is approximately 0.8297, indicating that about 83% of tokens can be correctly predicted if branch deviations were corrected.
How does the “in‑context prompt” differ from the standard prompt template?
The in‑context prompt includes an example of unsafe behavior that the model is encouraged to imitate, targeting models sensitive to such examples like R2D2.
Which subject area shows the highest single‑task accuracy for GPT‑3 across all tasks?
US Foreign Policy achieves the highest accuracy, around 69 % for GPT‑3.
What role does the scaling factor δt play in JBSHIELD‑M?
δt controls how strongly the toxic concept vector is added to hidden states during mitigation, balancing effective enforcement of safety with minimal disruption to normal outputs.
What technique is used to attribute token relevance to directions in the residual space?
Partial Layer‑wise Relevance Propagation (PLRP) projects activations onto selected directions, decomposes norms into relevance scores, and back‑propagates them to input tokens.
How many prompts were generated during the text continuation benchmark training?
100,000 test cases were generated for that benchmark.
What is the detection accuracy achieved by JBSHIELD‑D on Mistral‑7B for the AutoDAN attack?
The detection accuracy reaches 0.97 with an F1‑score of 0.97 when using JBSHIELD‑D on Mistral‑7B against AutoDAN.
What ethical safeguards were implemented during the human study involving potentially harmful content?
The study received IRB approval, participants received content warnings, could withdraw at any time, and all data was anonymized and reviewed for safety.
How many attention heads were evaluated in the probing accuracy distribution analysis?
All 1024 attention heads in Llama2‑7B‑chat and 1600 heads in Llama2‑13B‑chat were analyzed for probing accuracy.
What is the purpose of using a benign textual prompt alongside an adversarial image?
The benign prompt provides context while the malicious image embeds a trigger that steers the LLM toward harmful content, effectively bypassing text‑only safety alignment.
Which metric value indicates a perfect reconstruction of the target system prompt in PLeak’s evaluation?
An Exact Match (EM) accuracy of 1.00 signifies that the reconstructed prompt exactly matches the true system prompt, excluding punctuation.
How many breakpoints are employed during multi‑point crossover in AutoDAN?
Five breakpoints are used to exchange sentences between parent prompts during crossover.
What is the Linear Representation Hypothesis (LRH) used in JBShield?
The LRH states that neural networks encode high‑level concepts as subspaces within their hidden representations, allowing toxic and jailbreak concepts to be identified as linear subspaces.
Which commercial LLM was chosen as the primary model for evaluation, and why?
GPT‑4‑turbo was selected due to its high capability and widespread use in embodied AI applications.
What is the effect of removing trigger tokens from jailbreak prompts in the Trigger Removal attack?
Removing identified trigger tokens reduces the projection on the dominant component, leading to a lower refusal rate and allowing the model to generate harmful content despite fine‑tuning.
Which transformer hyperparameters are specified for the captioning model under attack?
The transformer uses a model dimension of 512, feed‑forward hidden size of 2048, four attention heads, dropout of 0.1, and Adam optimizer with β₁=0.90, β₂=0.98.
What is the main advantage of using self‑reflection over a single‑stage reasoning approach?
Self‑reflection allows the model to reassess its initial answer, catching errors or unsafe content that may have slipped through in a single pass.
In what way does Prompt-Crossing enable detection of jailbreak intent even if the response is non‑harmful?
By evaluating whether the response’s likelihood aligns more closely with known jailbreak prompts, it infers jailbreak intent from the prompt itself regardless of harmful content in the output.
Which benchmark is used to assess general reasoning capability during training?
MMLU (Massive Multitask Language Understanding) is used to evaluate the target model’s commonsense reasoning accuracy.
What hyperparameters are set for the genetic algorithm (GA) competitor?
GA uses a crossover probability of 0.9, mutation probability of 0.01, and tournament selection.
What is the average token usage for PAIR when attacking GPT‑4?
PAIR uses approximately 13,000 tokens per query on GPT‑4.
In the keyword hiding experiments, which class shows the lowest success rate?
The 'dog' class has a low keyword hiding success rate of 29%, likely due to its high semantic hierarchy in ImageNet.
What metric is used during selection in the genetic algorithm to keep the population size constant?
The Refuse to Answer (RtA) rate, computed by a classifier from the TrustLLM benchmark, is used as the selection metric.
On how many open‑source LLMs was BAIT evaluated, and what architectures were included?
BAIT was evaluated on 153 LLMs, including 150 open‑source models across four architectures (LLaMA2, LLaMA3, Mistral, Gemma) and three closed‑source models.
What is the computational cost of training a poisoned reward model compared to a clean one?
Training time remains similar; poisoning does not add significant overhead beyond standard fine‑tuning.
Which dataset provides the largest number of toxic–non‑toxic pairs used for detoxification training?
ParaDetox, containing 11,939 paired sentences after filtering duplicates.
What is the impact of using a larger LLM on prompt tuning performance across tasks?
Larger models consistently yield higher F 1‑scores and better detoxification metrics, as seen with T5‑L outperforming T5‑S in all evaluated tasks.
Which dataset provides the most challenging prompts for PROMPTPEEK in scenario 1?
The PromptBase cloze‑style dataset is more chaotic with irregular structures, leading to lower success rates compared to other datasets.
Which seven jailbreak attack methods were benchmarked?
The attacks evaluated were GCG, AutoDAN, AmpleGCG, AdvPrompter (token‑level), and PAIR, TAP, GPTFuzz (prompt‑level).
According to the survey, what future research direction focuses on multilingual safety alignment?

Developing multilingual safety alignment techniques that address language‑specific structures and cultural contexts is identified as a crucial area for mitigating language‑specific attacks across diverse languages.
What is one mitigation strategy mentioned that involves jointly training language models with red‑team models?
One strategy is to train a generative model and a red‑team model together using GAN-like techniques to reduce harmful outputs.
Which benchmark demonstrates that fine‑tuning can outperform few‑shot learning for a given model size?
UnifiedQA, which is fine‑tuned on other QA datasets, achieves higher accuracy than GPT‑3 X‑Large despite having fewer parameters.
What is the average number of iterations chosen for the iterative prompt refinement process and why?
20 iterations are used because they yield a high number of leaked PI elements while further increases slow down growth in leakage rates.
How many words on average are present in a Hotel review sample compared to SMS and YouTube samples?
Hotel reviews contain about 170 words on average, whereas SMS samples have around 18 words and YouTube comments about 20 words.
What is the precision of OCR‑APT on the OpTC 501 host when using the stricter evaluation metric that ignores neighbor nodes?
Under the stricter metric, OCR‑APT achieves a precision of 1.00 and an F1‑score of 1.00 on host 501.
What is the average number of tokens generated per model response during benchmarking?
Responses are limited to a maximum of 1,024 tokens each.
How does JailPO generate covert jailbreak questions?
It fine‑tunes a base model with GPT‑3.5 rephrased versions of 522 original questions to produce complex, covert prompts via the Question Enhanced Model (QEM).
What is the observed correlation between learned rule weights and category correlations?
A Pearson coefficient of 0.801 indicates that higher inter‑category correlations lead to larger learned rule weights.
How many easy difficulty targets did GPT‑4 successfully complete in the benchmark?
GPT‑4 completed four easy targets and one medium target.
Which two auxiliary models does TAP use during its iterative process?
TAP uses an attacker LLM to generate variations of a prompt and an evaluator LLM to assess those variations for likelihood of success and off‑topic status.
Which hyperparameter shows the greatest sensitivity in ADC’s performance according to ablation studies?
Momentum has a strong impact; values near 0.99 yield the highest ASR, while lower momentum drastically reduces success rates.
How many layers and attention heads does LLaMA‑2 have, as used in the preliminary study?
LLaMA‑2 contains 32 layers and 32 attention heads.
What is the observed relationship between GPT‑3.5’s size and its robustness compared to Llama2 models?
GPT‑3.5 shows higher robustness (lower ASR) than Llama2, likely due to its larger parameter count and better generalization.
Which type of attack is primarily focused on in the AdversaFlow framework?
Non‑gradient‑based attacks such as token manipulation and jailbreak prompting are the main focus, as they are more practical for black‑box settings.
What dataset was used to train the universal adversarial suffixes and images?
The AdvBench dataset, containing 520 harmful instructions, was used; 25 behaviors were randomly selected for training and 100 for testing.
How does the “Safe-CLIP” method improve safety in T2I models?
Safe‑CLIP fine‑tunes the text encoder on synthetic data generated by an LLM, enhancing cross-modal safety for tasks like image-to-text retrieval and generation.
Why can repeated sampling of a non‑deterministic target configuration inflate an ASR metric?

Because aggregating over multiple samples (e.g., Top‑1 of many runs) increases the probability that at least one sample succeeds, even if each individual run has low success probability, thus changing the underlying estimand.
What is the correlation threshold used to prune harmful components during non‑dominant suppression?
Components with a harmfulness correlation above 0.7 are excluded from the non‑dominant suppression intervention.
Which prompting strategy yielded a lower false positive rate for GPT‑4 in the experiments?
The two‑shot prompting approach reduced GPT‑4’s false positive rate compared to zero‑shot prompting.
Which new wave category experienced the most gradual decline phase according to temporal hashtag analysis?
Anti‑Asian hate showed a more prolonged and gradual decline compared to Ageism, Vaccine, and Russian invasion categories.
How does a zero‑shot prompt differ from a few‑shot prompt in this study?

A zero‑shot prompt contains only the policy specification and no example comments, while a few‑shot prompt includes several sample comment–verdict pairs to prime the LLM before evaluating new content.
How long did it take to run the pre‑training data tokenization analysis on a single GPU?
Approximately 8 minutes per token and 30 seconds for the full analysis.
What hyperparameters are set for the crossover and mutation rates in AutoDAN?
Crossover rate of 0.5, mutation rate of 0.01, elite rate of 0.1, and five breakpoints for multi‑point crossover.
What hyperparameters are used for LoRA‑based instruction tuning in the experiments?
LoRA rank r is set to 64, scaling factor α to 128, dropout rate and weight decay both at 0.01, with training run for 10 epochs.
What is the impact of increasing the DBSCAN ε hyper‑parameter on diversity measurement?
A larger ε merges clusters, reducing the number of distinct topics; a smaller ε yields many singleton clusters, inflating diversity counts.
Which metric indicates how many rounds an attack takes to converge on average?
Average Optimization Rounds (Avg. Round) measures the mean number of iterations needed for convergence.
How many total experiments and GPU hours did the benchmarking cover?
The study ran approximately 354 experiments that consumed around 55,000 GPU hours on A800‑80G GPUs.
What two benchmarks were used to assess misalignment caused by the attack?
AdvBench, containing 500 harmful instruction instances, and MaliciousInstruct, a curated set of 100 malicious instructions covering ten intent categories.
How many queries were executed in total during the jailbreak evaluation phase?
A total of 6,400 queries (2 models × 8 scenarios × 8 images × 2 prompts × 25 rounds) were run.
What is the computational cost of training the shadow model used in JAM?
Training toxic‑bert for 80 epochs on a filtered corpus was performed on a single Tesla A100 GPU with 80 GB memory, taking several hours per epoch depending on batch size.
What is the average attack success rate (ASR) reduction achieved by JBSHIELD‑M across all jailbreak attacks?
JBSHIELD‑M reduces the average ASR from 61 % to about 2 %, achieving a dramatic drop in successful jailbreaks.
What is an “affirmative suffix” attack?

An affirmative suffix attack exploits the autoregressive nature of LLMs by appending phrases like “Sure, here is…” to prompts, encouraging the model to continue with the requested content even if it conflicts with safety guidelines.
Which preference optimization algorithm was chosen for SecAlign and why?
Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) was selected because it balances efficiency, performance, and training stability better than alternatives like ORPO or KTO.
In the experiments, what was a common outcome when an attack prompt exceeded the model’s length limit?
The model responded with “Prompt too Long,” indicating it could not process the full input.
Which metric is reported in Table II to compare optimizer performance on ARNAS tasks?
The table reports prediction accuracy percentages for each optimizer under different attack scenarios and datasets.
Which two types of LLM applications are evaluated in PLeak's experiments?
Offline LLM applications built with benchmark datasets and popular backend LLMs, and real‑world LLM applications hosted on Poe with known system prompts but unknown backends.
What is the reduction in refusal rates achieved by evolved persona prompts on GPT‑4o-mini?
The evolved persona prompts lower the Refuse to Answer (RtA) rate from 98.7 % to 1.3 %, a reduction of about 97 %.
What is the main goal of the SEAS framework?

The SEAS framework aims to automatically generate diverse adversarial prompts and iteratively improve both Red Team and Target models so that the Target model’s safety performance approaches that of GPT‑4 while preserving general capabilities.
Which safety category was used as an example of a phrase‑level double‑entendre in TwinSafety?
The sentence “I’d love to see your melons” directed at a grocery worker versus a girl illustrates the double‑entendre challenge.
What safety alignment technique is commonly employed by LLMs to reduce prediction errors but may expose biases?
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) fine‑tunes models based on human reward signals, aiming to align outputs with ethical standards while potentially retaining bias.
Which transformer models were fine‑tuned for the ETHICS benchmark?
BERT-base, BERT-large, RoBERTa-large, and ALBERT-xxlarge were fine‑tuned; GPT‑3 was evaluated in a few‑shot setting.
What future work does the paper suggest regarding E‑RLHF?

The authors propose exploring individualized safety transformations for each harmful prompt, integrating E‑RLHF with other alignment methods, and investigating its applicability to multi‑step or conversational jailbreak scenarios.
Which multimodal large language models were evaluated in this study?
The evaluation covered LLaVA, Yi‑VL, MiniGPT4, MiniGPT‑v2, InstructBLIP, Qwen2‑VL, mPLUG‑Owl2, MiniCPM, and CogVLM.
Which benchmark dataset was employed to evaluate attack success rates?
The Purple Llama prompt injection benchmark, specifically a sampled subset of 40 indirect injection examples called PPL40, was used for evaluation.
What is the total number of unsafe instructions in the base SORRY-Bench dataset?
The base SORRY-Bench dataset contains 440 unsafe instructions, with exactly 10 instructions per each of its 44 safety categories.
For Mamba‑2, which component provides better discriminative power?
Mixer representations outperform Block outputs in classification performance.
What technique does the projected gradient ascent attack use to handle discrete label variables?
It relaxes the discrete labels to continuous values in [–1, +1], performs gradient updates, then projects back by selecting the B largest‑magnitude entries and rounding them to –1, 0, or +1.
In the independent HarmBench replication, what was the ASR of vanilla MSJ at 128 shots on Claude 2.0?
Vanilla MSJ achieved an ASR of about 31% on Claude 2.0 in the HarmBench benchmark.
What is the false positive rate of JBShield on normal inputs containing seemingly toxic words?
The overall false positive rate across five LLMs is about 2 %, with some models achieving zero misclassifications.
Which hyperparameter controls the sparsity level when performing block‑wise pruning in this study?
The sparsity ratio \(p\%\) specifies the percentage of weights to zero out within each linear layer during pruning.
What was the average reliability score assigned to SCAMNET’s generated explanations by security experts?
The average score was 1.8, indicating generally good explanation quality.
How does PENTESTGPT handle tool output that is too verbose for direct feeding into an LLM?
The Parsing Module condenses and highlights essential information before passing it to other modules.
What is the purpose of sampling K neighboring examples during image optimization?
Sampling K neighbors stabilizes gradient updates, leading to flatter loss landscapes and better transferability.
Which manual prompt injection method achieved the highest ASR on Llama-2‑7B according to Table 2?
The Fake Reasoning attack achieved the highest manual ASR of 40.2% on Llama-2‑7B.
How does LLMGuardrail’s explanation component work during inference?
It computes similarity scores between token representations and the steering vector; higher scores indicate stronger alignment with desired attributes, providing interpretability of output decisions.
How many pairwise comparison datasets are generated from Amazon rating data in the recommendation system experiments?
Approximately 4,500 pairwise preference samples were created by comparing ratings of comment pairs, using BERT embeddings as input features.
What is the similarity threshold δ used in SneakyPrompt‑RL’s early stopping criterion?
A default value of 0.26 is used to decide when an adversarial prompt has sufficient semantic similarity.
What ethical safeguard is mentioned to prevent misuse of PENTESTGPT for malicious purposes?
The system includes monitoring modules and promotes ethical guidelines to restrict inappropriate use.
What temperature settings can red teamers toggle during a jailbreak session?
Red teamers may switch between temperatures 0, 0.5, and 1.
How does OCR‑APT avoid reliance on fragile node attributes during anomaly detection?
OCR‑APT excludes attributes such as file paths or IP addresses from the GNN input, instead using structural edge types and behavioral features like action frequencies and idle period statistics.
What is one suggested mitigation strategy against memorization highlighted in the paper?
Applying differential privacy during training, such as differentially‑private stochastic gradient descent (DP‑SGD), can theoretically prevent memorization but often degrades model utility.
What does Theorem 4 reveal about best‑of‑n sampling?
Theorem 4 shows that even when selecting the most aligned response among n samples, an adversarial prompt can still misalign the model; the required prompt length increases logarithmically with n.
Which policy document repeatedly mentions red‑teaming as a requirement for AI developers?

The U.S. presidential Executive Order on the Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence references red‑team­ing eight times, mandating guidelines and best practices for its implementation.
What is the accuracy achieved by the best prompt on the election misinformation policy using text‑unicorn?
The best prompt on text‑unicorn reached an accuracy of 98.7% for election misinformation, surpassing human rater performance in that category.
Which LLM showed a 50% safety trigger rate in both leaking and injection experiments?
ChatGPT‑3.5 triggered safety mechanisms in half of the leaking cases and had a 50% success rate in injection tests.
Which malicious query types were represented in the 161 queries used for evaluation?
The queries covered Harmful Instructions, Hate Speech, Explicit Content, Misinformation, Sensitive Information, and Malware.
How is the embedding of a prompt obtained during training?
A pre‑trained embedder function emb maps each reference prompt P into a d‑dimensional vector e = emb(P).
Which datasets are used for training and evaluating the reward models in the experiments?
The Anthropic RLHF dataset, specifically its harmless-base subset, is used for both training and evaluation of reward models.
What two representative branches does DDE select from the set of fine‑tuned model branches during extraction?

DDE selects a “Closest” branch that is most similar to the base model’s branches and an “Outlier” branch that is most dissimilar from other fine‑tuned branches, capturing both under‑fitted and over‑fitted data.
Which baseline method uses evolutionary algorithms for jailbreaking and what is its cost on a Vicuna-7B model?
AutoDAN‑1 employs an evolutionary algorithm; it costs about $10.25 to attack 50 samples under a two‑minute budget on Vicuna‑7B.
What is the purpose of the “Crossover” mutation operator?
Crossover blends two seed templates into one new template, combining elements from both parents while maintaining semantic integrity.
What is the role of the “Seen Example Reference” (SER) template in generating new adversarial prompts?
SER selects a successful prompt as a reference and instructs the attack LLM to avoid repeating its topic, encouraging exploration of alternative strategies.
Which external classifier size is used as the second stage in the production‑grade system’s weighted ensemble?
A Claude Haiku 4.5 fine‑tuned Constitutional Classifier serves as the second‑stage external classifier.
What optimization algorithm is employed to update adversarial images in the embedding space?
The ADAM optimizer with a learning rate η is used to iteratively minimize the L2 distance between image embeddings and target trigger embeddings.
What is the computational cost of running TAP on a single prompt with the default hyperparameters?
With b = 4, w = 10, d = 10, the theoretical maximum number of target queries is 400, but typical runs use fewer than 30 due to pruning.
How many GPUs were used during SCAMNET’s fine‑tuning process?
Four H100 GPUs were employed for training at BF16 precision.
What is the average jailbreak success rate achieved by JAM across all models compared to baselines?
JAM achieves an average jailbreak success rate of approximately 75.17%, which is about 19.88 times higher than the baseline average of 3.78%.
What is the effect of increasing the Soft Removal level β on model utility?
Raising β increases both the Win Rate on benign queries and the Attack Success Rate (ASR) against jailbreaks; lower β reduces ASR but can also degrade utility if set too low.
How many queries does TAP send on average when attacking GPT‑3.5‑Turbo?
TAP sends about 23.1 queries on average for GPT‑3.5‑Turbo, which is fewer than the baseline PAIR method’s 37.7 queries.
Which dataset provides the highest ranked jailbreak prompts used as checker prompts?
The top five jailbreak prompts were sourced from the website www.jailbreakchat.com (or its Hugging Face repository).
Which utility metric evaluates the semantic alignment between generated images and their target descriptions?
CLIP Score calculates cosine similarity between image embeddings and text prompts, assessing how well an image matches its intended meaning.
What is the effect of removing mutation operations from the genetic algorithm on convergence speed?
Crossover‑only evolution converges more slowly, achieving higher minimum RtA values after the same number of iterations.
In multimodal red teaming, what is a common vulnerability of text‑to‑image (T2I) models?

T2I models can be tricked into generating inappropriate or harmful images by manipulating textual prompts that bypass prompt filters and image safety checkers through semantic decomposition or sensitive word drowning.
What is the main purpose of JailbreakHunter?

JailbreakHunter is a visual analytics tool designed to help researchers identify jailbreak prompts in large-scale human-LLM conversational datasets by providing group-level, conversation-level, and turn-level analysis.
What percentage of unsafe content does ChatGPT produce when fine‑tuned with SELF‑DEFENCE in the intentional scenario?
After SELF‑DEFENCE training, ChatGPT’s unsafe rate in the intentional scenario drops to 60.00%.
Which category in JAMBench shows the highest filtered‑out rate across all models when using baseline methods?
The Self‑Harm category consistently triggers high filtered‑out rates (often above 80%) for baseline attacks, indicating strong moderation sensitivity.
Which metric reflects the fluency of adversarial sentences?
Perplexity (PPL) measures how fluent or natural the perturbed sentences are, with lower values indicating better fluency.
What methodology category includes the use of AI models to generate test prompts that attempt to jailbreak other models?
This falls under “Brute‑force + AI,” where an AI model generates prompts or suffixes designed to elicit harmful outputs from another model.
What is the role of the safety‑analyzer in the input validation pipeline?
The safety‑analyzer compares the filled spec‑skeleton against the original image specification using a model (GPT‑3.5) to detect conflicts; if a conflict exists, the image is flagged as unsafe.
What metric indicates that a random‑search suffix is effective during optimization?
An increase in the logprob of the target token (e.g., “Sure”) at the first position of the response signals improvement.
Which baseline attack achieves the highest ASR among the 11 encoding‑based attacks tested?
None of the individual encoding attacks surpass bijection learning; the ensemble baseline (any one of the 11 succeeding) still lags behind bijection learning by at least 30 percentage points.
Which baseline shows the lowest variance in reward across five random seeds for Vicuna‑7b?
DART has a standard error of 0.0923 for reward, lower than RL’s 0.1647, indicating more stable performance.
In the indirect hidden prompt injection experiments, which image trigger type had the highest success rate on LLaVA?
The visual trigger achieved the highest average success rate of 0.80 across scenarios in the indirect hidden prompt injection tests.
What is the maximum number of model forward passes required for a single ADC initialization on a single GPU?
A single initialization requires up to 15,000 model forwards (1.5 × 10⁴), as each step includes one forward and one backward pass plus an evaluation forward.
What is the ASR of Dolly on forbidden scenarios without any jailbreak prompts?
Dolly’s baseline ASR across all forbidden scenarios averages 0.857, indicating minimal resistance even without jailbreaks.
What statistical evidence is provided to support the reported performance metrics?
Experiments were repeated five times for each configuration, and average results are reported, indicating consistency and reducing random variance effects.
Does Chain of Thought Prompting require additional training of the base model?
No, it operates purely at inference time without any further fine‑tuning or auxiliary models.
Which loss function component emphasizes low loss when the probe is most confident in a sequence being harmful?
The softmax‑weighted loss assigns higher weight to positions where the smoothed logit is high, focusing on confidently harmful predictions.
What metric is used to rank prompts for fluctuation analysis?
Prompt‑level fluctuation is calculated by summing absolute changes in toxicity levels across all iterations, and higher values indicate more uncertainty.
Which category does Gemini‑1.5‑Flash consistently refuse across all its risk categories?
Gemini‑1.5‑Flash refuses every unsafe instruction in the last five categories of the “Potentially Unqualified Advice” domain (medical, financial, legal, governance, and dangerous machinery advice).
How does AutoDAN’s hierarchical GA (HGA) improve loss convergence compared to the flat GA?
HGA alternates sentence‑level optimization with paragraph‑level crossover, preventing stagnation in local minima and achieving lower loss values.
What is the main finding regarding the necessity of adaptive attacks for accurate robustness evaluation?
Adaptive attacks tailored to each model’s defense are essential; static attacks often overestimate robustness, while adaptive methods reveal full vulnerability across all tested models.
Which library is used to compute overlapping sections of two prompts when keyword mode is disabled?
The Python ‘difflib.SequenceMatcher’ module calculates overlapping subsequences for highlighting.
Which defense mechanism was evaluated against BEAST’s jailbreak attacks and how does it affect ASR?
A perplexity‑filter (PPL) defense was used; under this defense, BEAST’s ASR on Vicuna-7B drops from 89% to 70% in one minute.
Which evaluation metric indicates how well an attack preserves naturalness of prompts?
