The heatmap displays 10 checker prompts across rows and columns, representing all combinations of 5 normal and 5 jailbreak prompts.
How many GPT‑3.5 rephrasings are generated per original question for QEM training?
Ten distinct rephrasings are produced per question to enrich the fine‑tuning dataset.
What cross‑validation strategy is employed to evaluate classification performance?
A five‑fold cross‑validation procedure is applied on the decomposed embeddings.
How does the integrated similarity guidance score differ from cosine similarity in guiding attacks?

Integrated similarity combines four cosine similarities—between adversarial and target class, original and target class, original and source class, and adversarial and source class—to more comprehensively guide word replacements.
How many turns were set for the multi‑turn dialogues in the preliminary study?
The dialogues consisted of five turns, with queries gradually increasing in toxicity from benign to harmful.
Explain the trade‑off between coverage and fidelity observed when applying CFG with α = 0.2 in the zero‑shot variant.
Increasing α to 0.2 raises fidelity from 0.475 to 0.498 but reduces coverage from 64.3% to 57.1% and drops Avg ASR from 21.1% to 12.6%.
Which one‑class SVM parameter controls the contamination factor in OCRGCN?
The contamination factor, denoted as γ, is set between 0.001 and 0.05 to reflect the expected proportion of anomalies while preventing overfitting.
How many unique defenses were included after filtering for reproducibility and diversity?
After filtering, 40 unique defense repositories remained, representing the core set used in evaluations.
What role does the dominant component play in model behavior according to the experiments?
The dominant component (first SVD vector) largely governs the model’s refusal behavior, predicting whether the model will refuse a harmful query.
What embedding model is used to generate instance embeddings for the projection plane?
The system uses the ‘all-mpnet-base-v2’ model from SentenceTransformers to compute embeddings of concatenated user queries.
Which datasets provided harmful instruction pairs for computing refusal directions?
Harmful prompts came from the AdvBench dataset, while harmless counterparts were sourced from the Alpaca dataset.
How does PLeak handle potential filter‑based defenses that remove exact sentences from responses?
By applying an adversarial transformation (e.g., adding a prefix), PLeak can evade sentence‑level filters and still recover the system prompt with EM around 0.30.
Which stage of the Chain of Thought Prompting is responsible for determining whether a user query is a jailbreak attempt?
Stage 4, Self‑Reflection, evaluates the response against potential jailbreak characteristics.
What is the average BPR achieved by MODX in Scenario #1 across four models?
The average BPR in Scenario #1 is approximately 0.92.
How many jailbreak prompts did MASTERKEY generate for evaluation against the target chatbots?
MASTERKEY produced 850 jailbreak prompts, with each prompt expanded into 10 variants during testing.
At what poisoning rate does a reward model’s accuracy on poisoned test data drop below 50% for both 7B and 13B models?
With 0.5% poisoned examples, accuracy falls to approximately 40–45%, well below 50%.
What is the reported ARR for geographically biased poem generation on the student model?
The poisoned student achieves an ARR of 89.0% for geographically biased poems, compared to only 0.3% for clean models.
What role does the reference model play in DPO training within SecAlign?
The reference model provides baseline logits that constrain the updated model’s output distribution, preventing it from deviating too far during preference optimization.
How many iterations does SEAS perform to reach its final safety level?
SEAS performs three full iterations of Attack and Adversarial Optimization stages before reporting that the Target model’s safety is comparable to GPT‑4.
What is the main advantage of applying adversarial noise to latent space rather than inputs?
It exposes hidden failure modes in deeper layers without needing specific adversarial examples at the input level.
What is the reported accuracy for the “DEV” user prompt category when using LLAMA as judge LLM?
The accuracy for DEV with LLAMA was 0.90.
What is the average performance of RoBERTa-large on the Hard Test set across all tasks?
RoBERTa-large achieves an overall average accuracy of 44.1% on the adversarially filtered Hard Test set.
How does the generation exploitation attack compare to the state‑of‑the‑art GCG attack in terms of ASR on LLAMA2‑7B‑CHAT?
The new attack achieves ASRs up to 99% on MaliciousInstruct using the classifier metric, whereas GCG reaches only about 50–60% under similar conditions.
How many parameters does the smallest UnifiedQA variant evaluated in the study contain?
The smallest UnifiedQA model used has 60 million parameters.
Which three characteristics become prevalent in evolved persona prompts over time?
Short sentences, rhetorical questions, and self‑deprecating humor are increasingly common as generations progress.
What is the purpose of the denoiser step after receiving an encoded response from the target model?
To correct minor decoding errors such as misspellings or extra tokens that may arise when the model outputs in the bijection language, ensuring accurate reconstruction of the original text.
How does LLMO update its best solution during each iteration?
After receiving Gemini’s response, LLMO checks feasibility, evaluates the returned architecture on the ARNAS instance, and if it outperforms the current best, updates the best solution accordingly.
How does R 2‑Guard adapt to new safety categories without retraining?
By adding a new category‑specific learning module and inserting corresponding nodes and rules into the PGM reasoning graph.
In retraining attacks, how does DDE’s performance compare to Vanilla and DSR when using 75% PSP preservation?
With 75% PSP, DDE achieves higher SFT performance than both Vanilla and DSR, sometimes surpassing models trained on the full dataset due to effective extraction of useful data.
What dataset is employed for training and validating the red‑teaming framework?
The Do‑Not‑Answer dataset is used for both training the models and evaluating their performance during red‑teaming.
In the transferability study for Task 1, which dataset’s prompts generalize best to other datasets?
Prompts trained on MHS transfer more effectively, achieving higher F 1‑scores on several target datasets compared to those trained on HateXplain.
How does suppressing non‑dominant components affect the model’s refusal accuracy on harmful prompts?
Suppressing non‑dominant components lowers refusal accuracy for harmful prompts (to about 80 %) while keeping benign refusal at 0 %, indicating that indirect features influence safety behavior.
What impact does the defense have on model performance under greedy decoding (temperature = 0)?
The defense leaves greedy decoding results unchanged, satisfying the design goal of preserving normal usage behavior.
What is the estimated cost difference between using a multi‑policy prompt and single‑policy prompts for evaluating 50,000 comments with text‑unicorn?
Using a multi‑policy prompt costs roughly $762, whereas single‑policy few‑shot prompts would cost about $1,137 for the same workload.
What are the three loss components used in JudgeDeceiver’s optimization objective?
The loss function includes a target-aligned generation loss, a target-enhancement loss, and an adversarial perplexity loss.
What is the main advantage of using an explainable LLM for fraudulent website detection mentioned in the paper?
Explainability reduces legal risk for browser vendors by providing clear justifications for blocking decisions.
What technique does PENTESTGPT use to reduce hallucination in the Reasoning Module’s outputs?
It verifies updated Pentesting Task Trees, ensuring only leaf nodes are altered.
Which safety category has the lowest representation in prior datasets compared to others?
Self‑harm instructions are over three times less represented than categories such as fraud or sexual explicit content in the ten evaluated prior datasets.
What attack success rate did UDora achieve on InjecAgent with Llama‑3.1 for data stealing?
UDora reached 99% ASR on Llama‑3.1, outperforming baselines by about 22%.
What user feedback indicated a steep learning curve for novice users?
Experts E1 and E5 expressed concerns that the system’s many functions could overwhelm new users, suggesting the need for more tutorials or guidance.
How did expert E5 identify multi‑turn jailbreak strategies in WildChat?

E5 created a filter selecting conversations that initially received non‑malicious responses but later produced malicious content, then brushed a cluster containing the keyword “horny” to examine repeated prompts.
Does the defense introduce any new safety risks or degrade model utility on benign inputs?
No; experiments show that with the secret prompt prepended during inference, benign question responses remain unchanged and accuracy on standard benchmarks is preserved.
How is a “dispersion” value of 0 interpreted in bijection learning?
A dispersion of 0 means every letter maps to itself, effectively sending the query in plain English.
What is the role of KeyBERT in the HATEGUARD pipeline?
KeyBERT extracts candidate targets and derogatory terms from seed tweets before novelty verification with WordNet.
How were refusal responses used in the ASR‑R calculation?
The model’s output was compared against a set of predefined refusal phrases; if the response did not match any refusal, it was counted as harmful for ASR‑R.
Which baseline attack method was adapted for LCCTs to demonstrate comparative effectiveness?
The CodeAttack study by Ren et al. was adapted; it originally targeted general LLMs but was re‑used as a benchmark against the new code‑based attacks on LCCTs.
How many layers are concatenated for the default all-layer probe used on Claude Sonnet 4?
Activations from all 46 layers of Claude Sonnet 4 are concatenated in the default probe.
In what way did the authors modify the bounty structure for the two-stage system’s red‑teaming campaign?
They increased the maximum reward to $35K and added a multiplier for each additional strong pass, incentivizing more detailed jailbreak responses.
What role does keyword context play in the few‑shot prompts?
Keyword context lists salient terms from violative examples; it can improve recall for certain policies but is unnecessary for text‑unicorn when accuracy is prioritized over recall.
What is the average discovery time for high-risk vulnerabilities found by the two-stage cascade system?
The mean discovery time was 33 hours for high‑risk vulnerabilities in the two-stage cascade.
How are the generator’s noise inputs structured before convolutional processing?
A 100‑dimensional uniform random vector is reshaped into an 8×8×256 tensor via a fully connected layer, then processed by transposed convolutions.
What hyperparameter was used for greedy decoding during all experiments?
The models were evaluated with greedy decoding (no temperature or top‑k sampling).
In cross‑model transferability tests, how does AutoDAN perform against Llama2‑7b‑chat compared to GCG?
AutoDAN achieves an ASR of 0.6077 versus GCG’s 0.4538, showing higher transferability to the robust Llama2 model.
What is the observed ARR for phishing link bias under targeted propagation in the student model?
The poisoned student achieves an ARR of 92.2% on phishing link bias when using targeted propagation, while non‑target tasks remain at 0%.
Which PEFT algorithm achieves the highest ASR increase on Llama when fine‑tuned with the HS dataset?
LoRA attains the largest ASR increase for Llama when fine‑tuned with the HS dataset, achieving over 80% ASR.
How many total jailbreak attempts were conducted per method during evaluation on JAMBench?
Each method was tested across all 160 questions in JAMBench, yielding a total of 160 jailbreak attempts per model per method.
In Table 6, which task shows the highest transferability from GPT‑3.5 to Llama2‑13B?
The MNLI‑mm task shows the highest ASR (81.51 %) when transferring from GPT‑3.5 to Llama2‑13B.
What is the flag rate observed in shadow deployment on production traffic for the production‑grade system?
The flag rate was 0.05%, meaning that 0.05% of queries were refused by the system.
Which hyper‑parameter setting yielded the highest ASR for LLAMA2‑13B‑CHAT when using varied top‑p sampling?
Setting p to 0.95 produced an ASR of 24% under the classifier metric, which was the best among top‑p configurations tested.
How many epochs were run during step one of SCAMNET’s supervised fine‑tuning?
Two epochs were executed in step one.
What adjustment did expert E1 make to balance security and general performance in Case I?
E1 increased the sampling rate for Information Hazards while reducing the proportion of Malicious Uses to improve overall model safety without harming reasoning accuracy.
What is the main limitation of the paper’s theoretical framework identified in the conclusion?

The framework relies on several strong assumptions (e.g., deterministic mapping from concept to support, bounded adversary capability) that may not hold fully in practice, limiting generalizability to more complex prompt structures or dynamic decoding strategies.
Which attack strategy achieved the highest overall success rate on the Hotel review dataset?
The greedy algorithm guided by integrated similarity achieved an 89.49% success rate on the Hotel review dataset.
How is harmfulness of a response evaluated during fuzzing?
A fine‑tuned RoBERTa model classifies each LLM output as harmful or not; a harmful classification yields a reward of 1, otherwise 0.
How many iterations were required for guided crossover to achieve an R₃ score of 0.9 during crossing experiments?
Guided crossover achieved an R₃ score of 0.9 in only two iterations, compared to four iterations without constraints.
Which component contributes more to improving attack success in black‑box scenarios according to the ablation results?
The transfer‑based strategy for the adversarial image has a larger impact than that for the suffix.
Describe the “Generate” mutation operator.
Generate instructs an LLM to produce a new jailbreak template in a similar style but different content, including a placeholder “[INSERT PROMPT HERE]”.
Which two target models were evaluated in the instruction‑following task?
GPT2‑alpaca and Dolly‑v2‑7B.
Which classifier achieves the best performance on Mamba‑2 Mixer output at middle layer 32?
SVM with RBF kernel attains a 94.2% F1 score at middle layer 32 Mixer output.
What role do ciphers play in generalization glide attacks?

Ciphers transform sensitive keywords into encoded forms (e.g., ROT13, base64) that bypass keyword filters while preserving semantic intent, allowing attackers to exploit the model’s ability to decode and respond appropriately.
How does the AutoJailbreak framework automate prompt refinement?
AutoJailbreak uses weak-to-strong prompt optimization, suffix-based enhancement, and efficient search to iteratively refine attack prompts with minimal human intervention.
What empirical effect does E‑RLHF have on attack success rates?

Across all HarmBench and AdvBench adversaries, E‑RLHF reduces the average ASR to 36.95 % and 20.89 %, respectively, outperforming standard DPO and RLHF without sacrificing helpfulness (MT‑Bench scores remain above 6.7).
What reinforcement learning architecture is used for policy network in SneakyPrompt‑RL?
An LSTM with a fully connected layer that outputs token probabilities sequentially.
Which policy experienced the largest drop in accuracy when all four policies were combined into one prompt?
Election misinformation suffered the largest accuracy decline, dropping by up to 13.7% compared to its single‑policy performance.
How many workers were required to annotate each Virtue Ethics scenario for trait labeling?
Three MTurk workers labeled each scenario, and only traits with unanimous agreement were kept.
What baseline method does PARDEN compare against in the ROC analysis?
It compares against a binary classification defense proposed by Helbling et al. and a perplexity filter from Jain et al.
How does the Overflow‑L attack differ from Overflow‑S in its prefix construction?
Overflow‑L inserts a longer, more varied prefix generated by an uncensored LLM, whereas Overflow‑S uses a fixed short phrase like “Sure, here is”.
What clustering evidence supports the two‑component mixture assumption?
Embedding space analysis showed that last‑token representations of positive and negative prompts cluster distinctly, with SVM classifiers achieving over 90 % accuracy across behaviors.
How does PARDEN avoid the auto‑regressive trap that many jailbreaks exploit?
By re‑processing the entire LLM output at once for repetition, PARDEN prevents the model from committing to harmful tokens early in generation.
How does Theorem 2 quantify jailbreaking probability?

The theorem bounds the probability that an ε‑bounded adversary finds a jailbreak prompt by \(\Phi(a\epsilon)\), where \(a\) depends on the sizes of harmful and safe explanation sets, and \(\Phi\) is the standard Gaussian CDF.
What is the paper’s stance on using LLMs as judges for evaluating jailbreak success?
The paper cautions that different LLM‑as‑judge models can produce large variations in ASRs and even reverse model rankings, indicating measurement validity concerns that must be addressed.
What cross‑entropy loss pattern was observed when user prompts matched the type of checker prompts?

Lower cross‑entropy losses were seen when a normal user prompt paired with a normal checker prompt or a jailbreak user prompt paired with a jailbreak checker prompt, indicating higher likelihood consistency.
How many sub‑tasks are defined across all benchmark targets?
There are 182 sub‑tasks distributed among the 13 targets.
Which large language model (LLM) was used to run HateCoT prompts in experiments?
GPT‑4 accessed via the official OpenAI API endpoints served as the LLM for HateCoT prompting.
Which external safeguard shows the smallest ASR reduction on GPT‑3.5 when applied to jailbreak prompts?
The NeMoGuardrails safeguard provides the smallest ASR reduction of 0.019 on GPT‑3.5 for the most effective jailbreak prompt.
Which five datasets were used for evaluating toxicity classification?
HateXplain, USElectionHate20, HateCheck, SBIC.v2, and MHS.
How does JailFuzzer handle non‑filter based defenses such as UCE, POSI, and SafeGen?

It adapts its mutation loop to detect whether generated images contain malicious content and continues mutating until semantic alignment is achieved, achieving bypass rates of 94%, 81%, and 82% respectively.
What is the sliding window size (M) used during training of linear probes, and why is it important?
The window size M is set to 16; it smooths logits over consecutive tokens to reduce false positives from isolated spikes while maintaining sensitivity to consistent harmful content.
What is the purpose of multiplying all reward components together in the final objective?
To ensure the attacker simultaneously satisfies success, similarity to examples, style diversity, and length constraints without neglecting any single component.
Which open‑source LLMs were evaluated with ADC on the AdvBench harmful behaviors subset?
The evaluated models are Llama2-chat-7B, Vicuna-v1.5-7B, Zephyr-7bβ, and Zephyr 7B R2D2.
Which two types of mutation are applied in CODEBREAKER’s dynamic mutation step?
Crossover mutation combines content words from high‑quality prompts, while dictionary mutation inserts or replaces words with common PI data such as names or passwords.
How many open‑source models were evaluated in the white‑box experiments?
Five open‑source models—Llama2‑7B, Llama2‑13B, Llama2‑70B, Vicuna‑7B, and Mistral‑7B—were used.
Which component of RLHF makes it harder for small poisoning attacks on the reward model to persist in the final aligned model?
The dual training paradigm—reward modeling followed by PPO finetuning—acts as a barrier, requiring more poisoned data to survive.
In the multi‑loop attack flow, what determines when a prompt is moved from the failed seed pool to the successful seed pool?
A prompt moves to the successful pool after it successfully bypasses safety filters and meets the semantic similarity criterion within the allowed mutation limit of that loop.
What is the main goal of ProAttacker?

ProAttacker aims to generate semantically consistent adversarial perturbations for text by guiding a masked language model with label-related prompts, thereby improving attack success rates and reducing perturbation rates.
What benchmark did the authors create to evaluate BADROBOT’s performance?
They constructed a benchmark of malicious physical action queries covering categories such as Physical Harm, Privacy Violations, Pornography, Fraud, Illegal Activities, Hateful Conduct, and Sabotage.
How many target queries are used in the red‑teaming protocol for evaluating jailbreak success?
Eight target queries spanning various harmful categories are used.
What is the primary difference between “Hidden Intention Streamline” and “Direct Request” tactics?
Hidden Intention Streamline uses a series of seemingly harmless turns that culminate in a harmful request, whereas Direct Request asks for the harmful content outright in one prompt.
How does the presence of a single space token influence the model’s first predicted token type for most models?
It shifts the first token from non‑numeric to numeric in many models, aligning with pre‑training patterns where spaces precede numbers.
Which distributed LLM framework is used as an example in the introduction?
The Petals framework is cited as a representative example that splits LLM layers between client and server modules for collaborative inference.
What are the reported fooling rates for Show‑and‑Tell when ε₁ = 0.1?
For ε₁ = 0.1, the exact‑match fooling rate is 53.9%, while the METEOR‑based success rate ranges from 71.3% to 90.1%.
What technique does Jailbreak-Zero use to increase diversity without fine‑tuning?
It references previously successful prompts as examples and instructs the attack LLM to generate new prompts on novel topics, preventing repetition of known strategies.
What metric did the Voting ensemble achieve on the JAILJUDGE dataset?
It achieved an accuracy of 0.86, a recall of 0.70, precision of 0.82, and F1 score of 0.76.
Which baseline jailbreak methods were compared against the persona prompts?
GCG, GPTFuzzer, Virtual Context, DAP, Al Ghanim et al., Translit, Chat‑NN, and PAP were used as baselines.
Which dataset size was used for training the Red Team model in SEAS?
The Red Team model was fine‑tuned on 16,000 entries from the SEAS training set, which contains a total of 18,000 prompts split into 16K training and 2K test examples.
Which metric measures how often the target model succeeds in refusing toxic prompts?
Attack success rate (ASR) counts successful refusals of toxic prompts relative to all prompts presented.
What is the effect of reducing training steps on GPT2‑Large performance?
The F 1‑score increases rapidly up to around 800 steps and then stabilizes near 0.731 at 2000 steps, showing diminishing returns after that point.
Which two new countermeasures does the paper propose against JAM, and how do they function?

The paper proposes Output Complexity‑Aware Defense, which flags responses with high linguistic complexity, and Secondary LLM‑based Audit Defense, which decodes ciphered outputs before applying moderation, both effectively eliminating JAM attacks.
What is the purpose of the GPT‑Recheck metric introduced in the study?
It uses an LLM to judge whether a response truly answers the malicious question rather than merely refusing, addressing false negatives in keyword detection.
What hyperparameters are set for Gradient Cuff in the main experiments?
The main experiments use μ = 0.02, N = P = 10, a temperature of 0.6, top‑p of 0.9, and a target false positive rate σ of 5%.
Which hyper‑parameter controls the trade‑off between computational efficiency and classification performance when using partial repeats?
The number of tokens \(n\) to repeat; increasing \(n\) improves AUC up to a point but also increases computation.
Which hyperparameters were kept default for the Llama‑2 fine‑tuning recipe?
The official Llama‑2 fine‑tuning settings from llamarecipes were used, including AdamW optimizer, learning rate 5e-5, batch size 8, and weight decay 0.01.
What malicious instruction was combined with non‑English prompts to create the intentional scenario?
The AIM (Always Intelligent and Machiavellian) instruction from jailbreakchat.com was concatenated with the translated harmful prompts to form the intentional queries.
What definition of memorization is used to evaluate whether a string is recoverable?
The paper defines “k‑eidetic memorization” as a string that can be extracted by the model and appears in at most k distinct training documents; a value of k=1 indicates the string occurs only once.
Which defense method shows the largest increase in ASR when an adaptive attack is applied?
Adaptive‑GCG increases ASR only marginally (≈0.1) on Vicuna-7B-V1.5, whereas other defenses see negligible changes, indicating Token Highlighter’s resilience to adaptive attacks.
Explain how the prompt template in LLMO indicates the objective to Gemini.

The prompt includes an “I” section stating that the objective is to maximize accuracy, and a “S” section detailing the number of operations and edges, guiding Gemini to produce solutions aimed at high accuracy.
Which evaluation metrics, besides METEOR, are reported for clean versus perturbed images in the transformer experiments?
BLEU‑3, BLEU‑4, and METEOR scores are reported to compare clean and perturbed image caption quality.
Which jailbreak community demonstrates the highest average closeness centrality in its graph representation?
The “Virtualization” community has the highest average closeness centrality at 0.975, indicating tightly connected prompts within that group.
What is the ASR achieved by ADC on Llama2-13B-chat in HarmBench standard behaviors?
ADC reaches an ASR of 56.5% on Llama2‑13B‑chat, outperforming other token‑level baselines.
How does the system compute token‑level fluctuation between safe and risky responses?
It calculates Euclidean distance between probability vectors of the first \(K\) tokens of a safe response and those of top‑\(n\) risky starting tokens, using the set of frequent risky starters.
Describe how PROMPTPEEK exploits the side channel created by LPM.
By observing that a matched candidate is served before non‑matched ones, the attacker infers whether its token matches the victim’s cache based on return order.
What is the average number of tokens added by BEAST’s adversarial suffix during a jailbreak attack?
The adversarial suffix length L is set to 40 tokens, so each attack appends 40 tokens after the user prompt.
What is an example of a conceptual incoherence issue identified in the paper regarding risk‑area comparisons?

Comparing ASRs across violation categories (e.g., adult content vs. crime) without ensuring that the prompt distributions for each category are comparable can lead to misleading conclusions about system vulnerability to specific risks.
Why do supervised finetuning (SFT) models fail to generalize a universal backdoor across unseen prompts?
SFT alone does not propagate the trigger effect beyond the specific poisoned examples, whereas PPO’s reinforcement learning generalizes the behavior.
What is the primary recommendation for service providers to mitigate KV cache side channels?
Obscure or randomize cache sharing behavior and restrict client control over request parameters to prevent attackers from observing cache hits.
Which reward function is used by the red‑team model to measure how undesirable a response is?
The reward function R(y) assigns a score based on the undesirability of the target model’s response y, often using a toxicity classifier.
Which attack class consistently required fewer computational resources in high‑dimensional settings?
The rank‑by‑distance (RBD) heuristics, especially those using simple distance metrics like Euclidean norm or reward difference, were faster and more scalable than gradient‑based methods.
How many most similar reported jailbreak prompts are shown in the Comparison View by default?
The Comparison View displays the top five (N = 5) most similar reported jailbreak prompts.
How does OCR‑APT handle multiple attacks that share system entities within a single subgraph?
OCR‑APT may merge such attacks into one subgraph; this can obscure boundaries but also captures shared infrastructure. Future work aims to segment subgraphs based on behavioral signatures.
What is the main objective of the “Unlearn” transformative defense at the generator level?
Unlearn removes harmful information from a model via targeted retraining while preserving performance on benign inputs, balancing safety and utility.
What metric is used to quantify MSJ success on malicious use‑case queries?
Success is measured by the frequency of harmful responses classified by a refusal classifier, and also by the negative log‑likelihood (NLL) of compliant completions.
What does Theorem 1 provide regarding pretraining?

Theorem 1 gives a non‑vacuous PAC‑Bayesian generalization bound that shows a well‑trained LLM will mimic harmful behaviour present in its training corpus, implying a high probability of generating harmful explanations for direct prompts.
What is the main difference between prompt engineering and transfer injection techniques in black-box jailbreak attacks?

Prompt engineering modifies textual prompts directly to mislead the model, while transfer injection converts malicious text into other modalities (e.g., images or audio) that carry the same harmful intent.
How many samples were used when evaluating prompt tuning with only 500 training examples per dataset?
Five hundred randomly selected samples for each dataset, except USElectionHate20 which was excluded due to its small size.
What is the average BLEU score observed for benign outputs after repetition according to Figure 3?
The BLEU scores cluster around 1.0, indicating near perfect preservation of benign content.
What attack success rate (ASR) does the Red Team model achieve against Llama‑3‑70B‑Instruct after three SEAS iterations?
The Red Team model reaches an ASR of 73.97 % against Llama‑3‑70B‑Instruct in the third iteration.
Which real‑world Poe application achieved an EM accuracy of 0.68 when attacked with multiple AQs by PLeak?
Across the evaluated Poe applications, a subset reached 0.68 EM accuracy using multiple adversarial queries from PLeak.
What type of compute resources are required to run a full ADC experiment on a single model?
Experiments were conducted on an NVIDIA A100 GPU with sufficient memory for the chosen LLMs, using 1‑2 GB of VRAM per forward pass and completing within minutes per sample.
Which attack families are included in the HarmBench suite used for evaluation?
HarmBench includes Direct Request, GCG, GBDA, AP, SFS, ZS, PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN, PAP‑top5, and Human jailbreak templates.
Which refusal phrases are used by the binary classifier to detect successful jailbreaks?
Phrases such as “I’m sorry”, “I cannot”, “not legal”, “unethical”, and similar expressions indicate a refusal.
What role does the factor matrix B play in CP decomposition for this application?
Factor matrix B captures latent dimensions across token embeddings, contributing to the overall representation of prompts.
In out-of-domain experiments, how does LLMGuardrail compare to SFT on the Black demographic group in ToxiGen?
LLMGuardrail achieves a refusal rate of 90.73% and toxicity of 8.13%, outperforming SFT’s 68.55% refusal and 31.45% toxicity.
Which two state‑of‑the‑art frameworks are highlighted for using KV cache sharing?
SGLang and vLLM both implement KV cache sharing to reduce memory usage and computation time.
What model was used for safety fine-tuning in the study?
The Llama 3 8B Instruct model was fine‑tuned using both Safety Supervised Fine‑Tuning (SSFT) and Direct Preference Optimization (DPO).
Which model is chosen as the jailbreak judge for evaluations?
Llama‑3‑70B with a custom system prompt is selected as the primary judge.
How does HATEGUARD handle non‑textual modalities according to its future work plans?
Future extensions aim to incorporate multimodal scenarios, such as images and memes, into the framework.
What OWASP list is fully represented in the benchmark’s vulnerability coverage?
The benchmark covers all vulnerabilities listed in OWASP’s Top 10 project.
Which trigger strings were tested beyond “SUDO” to assess generality?
Tokens like “$”, two‑token triggers, descriptive phrases such as “BeHarmfulNow”, multiple question marks, and long tokens like “SuperGodModeActivated”.
What is the computational advantage of PromptAttack over traditional adversarial sample generation methods?
PromptAttack requires only a few queries to the victim LLM, avoiding expensive fine‑tuning or large‑scale data processing.
What limitation is noted regarding the generalizability of the findings to other model architectures or scales?
The experiments were limited to Llama‑2‑7B‑chat, so applicability to larger models or different architectures remains untested.
How many benign behaviors are included for sanity checks in JBB-Behaviors?
100 benign behaviors matching each harmful behavior’s topic are provided.
Does selecting only the most harmful or topic‑specific conversations for poisoning significantly reduce the required amount of data?
No; oracle or narrow attacks perform similarly to random poisoning, showing no substantial advantage.
What template is used to format inputs for the victim LLM during inference?
Inputs to the victim LLM follow the template: “{user prompt} {user question}”.
Which open‑source code repository hosts the implementation of ADC?
The code is available at https://github.com/hukkai/adc_llm_attack.
Which safety data filter demonstrates the highest accuracy for detecting unsafe content in D unsafe corpus?
The OpenAI Moderation API achieves the highest detection accuracy on the D unsafe corpus among the evaluated filters.
Which dataset construction technique was used to increase the success rate of jailbreak prompts for evaluation?
Prompt injection was employed, inserting tokens after the user‑assistant separator to prime the model and improve attack success.
How many epochs and what learning rate were used in fine‑tuning for generation‑aware alignment?
Fine‑tuning ran for three epochs with a learning rate of 2×10⁻⁵, using a cosine scheduler and a warm‑up ratio of 0.3.
How many iterations are performed when generating a universal adversarial image in Algorithm 1?
The algorithm runs for T = 50 iterations.
Which two common alignment methods are focused on in the study?
The study focuses on reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) fine‑tuning and prompting with aligning prefixes as the primary alignment approaches examined.
How is the sample mean fθ(x) used in practice to approximate the expected refusal loss?
In practice, fθ(x) is calculated by sampling N independent responses from the model for query x, applying JB(·) to each response to obtain Y_i, and taking the average of these binary values.
Which discriminative input-level defense measures entropy gaps between image regions?
Intra-Entropy Gap analyzes entropy differences across non-overlapping image or text regions to detect stealthy manipulations.
What is the average token count of a jailbreak prompt compared to a regular prompt?
Jailbreak prompts average about five hundred fifty‑five tokens, roughly one and a half times longer than regular prompts.
What categories of content were most frequently memorized by GPT‑2?
The largest categories included US and international news (109), log files and error reports (79), license and terms of use documents (54), and forum or wiki entries (53).
What future research direction does the paper suggest regarding prefix selection for extraction attacks?
Developing better strategies to choose or generate prefixes that trigger memorized outputs could uncover more memorized data and improve attack effectiveness.
Which dataset did the authors use to generate 1,000 unique user queries for each application during SysVec training?
They used OpenAI’s GPT‑4o model with a temperature of 0.6 to produce diverse questions for each of the five selected applications.
How does a direct prompt injection differ from an indirect one?
Direct injection feeds malicious instructions straight to the model, while indirect injection embeds them in data the model consumes, such as a webpage.
How did the authors ensure that their jailbreak prompts remained applicable across different LLM chatbots during dataset building?
They replaced model‑specific terms (e.g., “ChatGPT”) with generic placeholders such as “you” or “developer”.
How many prompts were used as the benign prompt set \( \tilde{X} \) in the experiments?
The benign prompt set consisted of 20 clean prompts sampled from the validation dataset for each model during scanning.
How many harmful examples were included in the comprehensive dataset used for ablation evaluation?
The dataset contained 420 harmful examples from AdvBench plus 100 GPT‑4‑generated examples, totaling 520.
How many samples were included in the preference dataset constructed for training?
The preference dataset consisted of 2,600 samples, combining harmful jailbreak examples with harmless samples from OR‑Bench.
What is the GPU cost for training SecAlign with DPO on Llama‑7B using four 80 GB A100s?
Approximately two GPU‑hours per epoch, totaling about eight GPU‑hours for three epochs.
What is the definition of an adversarial prompt in this work?
An adversarial prompt is one that bypasses a safety filter (F(M,p)=0) and whose generated image has similar visual semantics to the image from the original sensitive prompt.
What is the average number of queries TAP sends to GPT‑4 o per prompt?
TAP averages 16.2 queries per prompt when attacking GPT‑4 o, which is a 60% reduction compared to PAIR’s 40.3 queries.
What is the primary difference between the Justice and Deontology tasks regarding what models predict?
Justice predicts whether a justification for unequal treatment is reasonable; Deontology predicts whether an exemption or role responsibility is reasonable.
Which black-box output-level attack estimates gradients using zero-order optimization?
Zer0-Jack and DiffZOO estimate gradients via zero-order methods to optimize adversarial prompts or images without accessing model internals.
What evidence supports that SKEWACT reaches minima in wide convex regions rather than narrow ones?
Figure 2 shows SKEWACT’s optimization path converging to a minimum in a wide convex region, while GCG skips several local minima in narrow regions.
What is the purpose of the few-shot similarity reward in the RL training loop?
It encourages the attacker to produce attacks that are not too dissimilar from a provided example, helping prevent collapse to identical outputs.
What is the average ASR reduction achieved by the OpenAI moderation endpoint when applied to the most effective jailbreak prompt?
The OpenAI moderation endpoint reduces the ASR of the best jailbreak prompt by 0.431, the largest reduction among the three evaluated external safeguards.
What is the difference between a “one‑shot” and a “Top‑1” ASR metric?
A one‑shot metric evaluates a single generated response per prompt, while a Top‑1 metric selects the best among multiple sampled responses or transformations, potentially inflating success rates.
What is the Adversarial Response Rate (ARR) metric used to measure bias success?
ARR measures the fraction of model outputs that contain the targeted adversarial content, such as the word “Gibble” for advertisement bias, over a set of test samples.
In the experiments, which baseline defense achieved the lowest PLS score against the Ignore‑Remember attack on Llama‑3‑8B‑Instruct?
SysVec reduced the PLS score to 3.56, lower than all other baselines for that attack.
What hyperparameter setting is used for the few‑shot prompts presented to GPT‑3?
Each prompt begins with a statement of the subject, includes up to five demonstration examples with answers, and ends with “Answer:” before the target question.
What is the primary security concern associated with LCCTs using proprietary code datasets for fine‑tuning?
Proprietary training data may contain sensitive user information from public repositories, leading to potential privacy leakage when models generate completions that echo that data.
What is the primary vulnerability exploited by fun-tuning attacks?

Fun‑tuning attacks exploit the fine‑tuning interface of proprietary LLMs, using the training loss returned after a single fine‑tune step with a very small learning rate to approximate log probabilities and guide prompt injection optimization.
How does the paper define “attack success probability” (α) in formal terms?
α is defined as the expected value of the judge’s indicator over the distribution of base prompts: α = E_{P∼D}[J(L(P); P)=1].
How does the study validate that the attack works across different model families?
Experiments with Qwen2-7B students distilled from Qwen-14B teachers and cross‑family distillations (Gemma teacher to Qwen student) show similar or higher ARR, confirming generality.
How many harmful requests from AdvBench are used in the evaluation set?
Fifty distinct harmful requests curated by Chao et al. form the evaluation set.
What metric is used to measure how much of a prompt was successfully extracted?
The reversal ratio (RR), calculated as the length of the extracted prompt divided by the total prompt length.
What is the primary difference between the toxic concept and the jailbreak concept as defined in JBShield?

The toxic concept represents harmful semantics present in both harmful and jailbreak prompts, while the jailbreak concept captures additional semantic cues that shift the model from refusal to compliance.
Which models were included in the scalability evaluation beyond the primary four models?
Additional models tested for scalability include Craiyon v4, FLUX v1, DALL·E 2, Imagen 2, Midjourney v4, and Stable Diffusion XL.
What ethical safeguards were implemented during the annotation of hateful content on Amazon Mechanical Turk?
Workers received warnings about potential hateful material, only those with a 90% approval rating and 1,000 approved HITs participated, and highly offensive words were censored in shared samples.
What is the significance of the “systematization” step in the measurement framework described?
Systematization defines the precise success criterion s and goal distribution D, turning abstract safety concepts into concrete, testable estimands that can be measured by ASRs.
Which machine unlearning defense was evaluated for its robustness against human jailbreaks?
The RMU (Robust Machine Unlearning) defense was evaluated.
What is the mean BLEU score for malicious outputs after repetition as reported in Figure 3?
The mean BLEU score for malicious examples is around 0.435 with a standard deviation of about 0.157.
How is the attack success rate (ASR) defined in this study?
ASR measures the proportion of prompts that lead to unsafe outputs; it is calculated using either refusal response matching (ASR‑R) or a moderator LLM assessment (ASR‑M).
What assumptions are made about the generative distribution over prompts \(D_P\)?
It is assumed that \(\text{supp}(D_P)\subsetneq \text{dom}(p_{\text{world}})\), meaning some plausible prompts never appear in the training corpus but are still semantically meaningful.
How is semantic entropy calculated for a prompt in CODEBREAKER?
Semantic entropy is computed by generating multiple responses, vectorizing them with TF‑IDF, clustering with DBSCAN, and applying Shannon entropy over the cluster sizes.
What evidence supports that training loss correlates linearly with average logprobs?
Empirical plots (Figure 2) show proportional growth of both metrics with output length, and regression experiments (Figure 3) yield R² values approaching 1 for long outputs.
What is the effect of increasing the total number of queries q on the performance of Gradient Cuff, according to the ablation study?
Increasing q improves both TPR and FPR control; fixed‑N strategies (increasing P) yield greater performance gains than fixed‑P strategies for a given query budget.
How many epochs are used in the lower‑level prompt tuning phase of Algorithm 1?
The lower‑level optimization runs for \(E_2\) fine‑tune steps, where \(E_2\) is a hyperparameter set by the experimenter (specific value not disclosed).
How does the number of shots affect the rate of insulting responses on Claude 2.0 according to Figure 6R?
The rate increases steadily over a span of up to 205 shots with no obvious sign of diminishing returns, indicating that longer contexts continue to enhance jailbreak success.
What is the Fine‑tuning based Jailbreak Attack (FJAttack) described in the paper?
FJAttack is a method where an attacker supplies a small set of harmful examples to the fine‑tuning process, causing a safety‑aligned LLM to lose its refusal behavior and generate unsafe content.
What percentage of prompts in the alpaca dataset remain unmodified by the DART method with ε=0.1?
The ASR is 2.27%, meaning that only about 2% of prompts were successfully turned toxic while staying within the proximity budget.
Which attack method achieved the lowest ASR among all evaluated methods for Amazon Q?
The Level II Code Embedded Attack had a 22.3% ASR on Amazon Q, the lowest among the listed attacks for that tool.
What is the role of the “MACE” defense in generator-level pruning-based strategies?
MACE identifies concept-correlated neurons responsible for undesirable concepts and prunes them to eliminate unsafe content while maintaining model performance.
Which metric indicates semantic preservation in detoxification experiments?
Embedding similarity scores, specifically SIM(F) and SIM(W), comparing original and detoxified sentences.
Which two large language models were evaluated with PARDEN according to the paper?
The evaluation was performed on Llama‑2‑7B and Claude‑2.1.
Which LLM exhibited a “Prompt too Long” failure when using the DAN approach?
Microsoft Copilot flagged prompts as too long under the DAN attack strategy.
What automatic toxicity classifiers were used to evaluate generated outputs?
Perspective API, BERT‑based toxic classifier, and RoBERTa‑based toxic classifier were employed for automatic evaluation.
Which dataset was used for safety alignment experiments with LLaMA-7B?
The PKU-SafeRLHF-30k dataset, containing 30k question-answer pairs annotated for helpfulness and harmlessness, was used; only harmlessness labels were employed.
What proportion of the test’s 57 tasks are classified under “Other” in the supercategory list?
Approximately one‑fifth (around 11–12) of the tasks fall into the Other supercategory, including professional medicine and business ethics.
What is the purpose of the ngram_match function in lexical constraints?
The ngram_match function approximates BLEU‑n to penalize or reward the presence of specific keywords or phrases within the generated attack, enabling precise control over lexical content.
How does the filtered‑out rate of JAM compare to that of baseline methods on JAMBench?
JAM maintains a low filtered‑out rate of roughly 10.21%, which is about six times lower than the baseline average of 54.76%.
What technique is used to recover the permutation of losses returned by the fine‑tuning API?
A single‑query approximate method constructs progressively corrupted target strings; the resulting ascending loss values reveal an approximate permutation with about 90% pairwise ordering accuracy.
What is the primary objective of a promotion attack in preference poisoning?
A promotion attack aims to increase the learned reward model’s value for a set of target outcomes so that these targets are preferred over other options when compared by the reward function.
What evidence supports that E‑RLHF does not degrade helpfulness?

The MT‑Bench scores for E‑DPO (6.7) are comparable to or slightly lower than the DPO baseline (6.9), indicating that expanding the safety zone does not significantly harm the model’s ability to provide useful responses.
Which red‑teaming methodology involves using image prompts combined with text queries to increase harmful output likelihood in multimodal models?
This approach is part of “Brute‑force + AI,” where an image (e.g., a bomb) paired with a query like “how to build a bomb?” improves the chance of generating harmful text.
What key insight does the paper provide about the relationship between base model selection and DDE effectiveness?

While using the exact base model that produced the fine‑tuned model yields optimal results, DDE remains effective with alternative base models, indicating robustness to variations in base model availability.
Which evaluator type was historically predominant but has fallen to third place in adoption over time?
Human Annotation has consistently been used early on but now ranks third due to high cost and time requirements.
Which attack method combines prefix injection, refusal suppression, and Base64 encoding?
The automated red teaming approach by Wei et al. used this combination against safety‑trained LLMs.
Which dataset is used for generating the preference dataset when the SFT dataset and preference dataset come from different domains?
The Cleaned Alpaca dataset is used for the preference dataset while the SFT model may be trained on a private commercial instruction‑tuning dataset, showing cross‑domain robustness.
How many epochs does the generator train for, and what optimizer is employed?
The generator trains for 300 epochs using the Adam optimizer with a learning rate of 1e‑4.
What metric is used to measure the quality of the converged point in the optimization process?
Average Converged Loss (Avg. Loss) measures how low the loss is at the final optimized trigger.
What is the role of the “llm-as-a-judge” component in OCR‑APT’s pipeline?
The judge LLM selects the most critical indicators of compromise from the comprehensive report and retrieves contextual subgraphs to enrich the final narrative.
What is the maximum number of sentence‑level iterations relative to paragraph‑level iterations in AutoDAN?
Sentence‑level iterations are set to five times the number of paragraph‑level iterations, ensuring fine‑grained word optimization before broader sentence recombination.
Which text‑to‑image models were evaluated against JailFuzzer in the experiments?
Stable Diffusion v1.4 (SD1.4), Stable Diffusion XL Refiner (SDXL), Stable Diffusion 3 Medium (SD3), and OpenAI’s DALL·E 3 were tested.
Which defense baseline was shown to be ineffective against GCG attacks when applied to SysVec?
Both perplexity‑based (PPL) and paraphrasing defenses performed poorly, with high PLS scores compared to SysVec’s low scores under GCG.
How does increasing the shadow dataset size affect PLeak’s EM and SM scores?
Expanding the shadow dataset from two to eight samples raises EM from about 0.401 to 0.728 and SM from roughly 0.641 to 0.992, improving attack performance.
How many adversary scenarios are defined in the attack model?
Three adversaries (A1, A2, A3) are defined, each with different query budgets and auxiliary data availability constraints.
